In the create loop of createBeadsFromVBriefPromise (src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts ~:378-402), when `bd create` throws (timeout) OR returns empty stdout, recover the ID before recording failure: re-query `bd list --json -l <issueLabel>` and select the bead whose title strictly equals the full title `${plan.id}: ${item.title}`. If exactly one matches, set beadIds.set(itemId, recoveredId) and push(fullTitle) into created, then continue so later items' --deps resolve. If none match, record the failure as today (genuine create failure). If more than one matches, push an explicit ambiguity error and do NOT set beadIds for that item. The re-query should use the adaptive timeout from resolveBdTimeout (and may use retryBd).
- AC: Given a bd create that times out but a subsequent bd list returns a bead whose title equals `${plan.id}: ${item.title}`, beadIds gains that item's recovered ID and a dependent item created afterward includes the recovered ID in its --deps argument.
- AC: Given a bd create timeout AND the re-query returns no bead with the matching full title, the item is recorded in errors and beadIds has no entry for it.
- AC: Given the re-query returns two beads with the matching full title, the function pushes an ambiguity error naming the title and does NOT set beadIds for that item (no silent pick).
- AC: Given a bd create that returns a non-empty bead ID, the normal path is taken and no recovery `bd list` is issued for that item (assert no list call follows a successful create).
close_reason: Implemented recoverBeadIdByTitle helper: after bd create throws or returns empty stdout, re-query bd list by issueLabel and match title === ; exactly one match recovers the ID, ambiguity/no-match surfaces an error. Added AC1-AC4 tests. Inspection PASSED.

title: pan-1813: Bounded exponential-backoff retry for idempotent bd reads (create excluded)
description:
Add a retryBd(fn, { attempts = 3, baseDelayMs = 500 }) wrapper in src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts that retries fn on failure with exponential backoff (delay = baseDelayMs * 2^(attempt-1)) and surfaces the last error after attempts are exhausted. Apply it to the idempotent bd ops in the create/dedup path ONLY: the connectivity bd ping (beads.ts:205/:229), the dedup bd list (listBeadsForIssue at :77, used by clearBeadsForIssue), the post-delete bd list (:108 path), and bd delete (:97). Do NOT wrap bd create. Sleep via `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms))` (no execSync sleep).
- AC: Given the post-delete bd list times out on attempts 1 and 2 then succeeds on attempt 3, clearBeadsForIssue completes with no error (reproduces and recovers issue Run 3).
- AC: Given bd list times out on all 3 attempts, the operation fails surfacing the timeout error and issues no 4th attempt (assert mockExecAsync list-call count === 3).
- AC: Given a single bd create timeout, no second create is issued — bd create is never wrapped by retryBd (assert mockExecAsync create-call count === 1).
- AC: The backoff unit test drives delays with vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync() and contains no real-time waits (no maxForks:1 workaround).
close_reason: Implemented retryBd helper with bounded exponential backoff (attempts=3, baseDelayMs=500). Wrapped idempotent bd ops: ping, listBeadsForIssue (dedup/post-delete/recovery lists), and delete. bd create excluded. Added AC1-AC4 tests with fake timers per repo rule. Inspection PASSED.

title: pan-1813: Stamp plan.status=proposed only after beads creation succeeds
description:
In planFinalizeCommand (src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts), move the `const canonicalFilename = stampPlanForFinalization(planPath, issueId)` call (currently line 167, before createBeadsFromVBrief at line 173) to AFTER the beads-success check (after line 194, before promotePlanning at line 225). On the beads-failure exit path (lines 176-190) the stamp must NOT run, so the workspace .pan/spec.vbrief.json keeps its pre-finalize plan.status. Keep canonicalFilename in scope for the success-path JSON/text output and ensure emitAutoPromotePhase ordering stays coherent. Do not change stampPlanForFinalization itself.
- AC: Given createBeadsFromVBrief fails, the workspace spec plan.status is NOT changed to 'proposed' (retains its pre-finalize value) and planFinalizeCommand exits non-zero.
- AC: Given createBeadsFromVBrief succeeds, plan.status is set to 'proposed' and canonicalFilename is stamped before promotePlanning is invoked (unchanged success-path behavior).
- AC: Re-running pan plan finalize after a prior beads failure produces one clean finalize — dedup clears any partial beads and plan.status flips to 'proposed' exactly once, with no manual reconciliation.
close_reason: Moved stampPlanForFinalization in planFinalizeCommand to after the beads-success check and before promotePlanning. Beads failure now exits with code 2 before stamping, leaving plan.status at pre-finalize value. Added AC1-AC3 tests in plan-finalize.test.ts. Inspection PASSED.

title: pan-1813: Adaptive, configurable bd op timeout routed through all bd calls in beads.ts
description:
Add a resolveBdTimeout(workspacePath) helper to src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts. Behavior: if process.env.PANOPTICON_BD_TIMEOUT_MS parses to a positive integer, return it verbatim WITHOUT probing. Otherwise run `bd ping --json` once (using a fixed bootstrap timeout constant BD_PING_BOOTSTRAP_TIMEOUT_MS = 8000 — the resolver must NOT call itself), read total_ms from the JSON, and return Math.min(180000, Math.max(30000, total_ms * 20)). If the ping throws or its JSON is unparseable, return the floor 30000 (never throw). Cache the resolved value for the lifetime of one createBeadsFromVBriefPromise call so the probe runs once per finalize. Replace the hardcoded operational timeout literals — bd list (beads.ts:80), bd delete (:98), bd create (:382), and the init/doctor/config recovery ops (:179,:183,:220,:238,:242,:206,:230) — with the resolved value. Leave the non-bd `which bd` check (:155, timeout 5000) and the resolver's own bootstrap ping unchanged.
- AC: Given PANOPTICON_BD_TIMEOUT_MS=120000, resolveBdTimeout returns exactly 120000 and does NOT invoke bd ping (assert mockExecAsync received no ping call).
- AC: With no env override: bd ping total_ms=5000 ⇒ resolveBdTimeout returns 100000; total_ms=255 ⇒ returns 30000 (clamped to floor); total_ms=12000 ⇒ returns 180000 (clamped to ceiling).
- AC: When bd ping throws or returns unparseable JSON, resolveBdTimeout returns 30000 and does not throw.
- AC: The bd create call in beads.ts passes the resolved timeout — a unit test asserts the timeout option mockExecAsync received for `bd create` equals the resolved value, and grep finds no remaining 15000/10000/30000 literal on the list/delete/create calls.
close_reason: Implemented adaptive configurable bd op timeout via resolveBdTimeout(): env override wins, else bd-ping probe with floor/ceiling clamp; routed all bd operational timeouts in beads.ts; added AC1-AC4 tests. Inspection PASSED.

title: pan-1824: Add a test:slow npm script so the excluded @slow set stays runnable on demand
description:
Add a `test:slow` script to package.json `scripts` defined as `vitest run --tagsFilter @slow` (the inverse of the default `!@slow` exclusion; --tagsFilter is the verified vitest 4.1.6 CLI flag). This keeps the @slow set runnable on demand rather than silently orphaned. Note the @slow UAT uses real chromium.launch(), so a green run requires chromium installed (`npx playwright install chromium`); the bead's AC only requires that the script SELECTS the @slow test for execution, not that the browser test passes in every environment. Place the script next to the existing `test` / `test:unit` entries.
- AC: package.json `scripts` contains `test:slow` defined as `vitest run --tagsFilter @slow`
- AC: When `npm run test:slow` runs, vitest selects the @slow-tagged UAT test for execution rather than reporting zero matched tests (the inverse of the default exclusion)
- AC: `npm run lint` passes with the new script present (no JSON or skills-lint drift)

title: pan-1824: Tag the conversation-supervisor Playwright UAT @slow and prove the default run excludes it
description:
In tests/playwright/conversation-supervisor-uat.test.ts, add the @slow tag to the single describe at line 401, replaying the known-good hunk from `git show d9807686d -- tests/playwright/conversation-supervisor-uat.test.ts`:

    -describe('conversation supervisor Playwright UAT', () => {
    +describe('conversation supervisor Playwright UAT', { tags: ['@slow'] }, () => {

Then prove exclusion: run the default `npx vitest run tests/playwright/conversation-supervisor-uat.test.ts` and confirm zero tests execute (filtered by tagsFilter `!@slow`), and that a default `npm test` no longer runs 'delivers through real conversation routes…'. Local verification is a faithful CI proxy here because tag filtering is deterministic config behavior, independent of whether tmux/IO ops succeed (unlike the reverted fake-timer conversion). Touch ONLY this test file; do NOT convert it (or its neighbors) to fake timers.
- AC: The single `describe('conversation supervisor Playwright UAT', ...)` is given `{ tags: ['@slow'] }` as its second argument
- AC: When `npx vitest run tests/playwright/conversation-supervisor-uat.test.ts` runs with the default config, it executes zero tests (the file's test is filtered out by tagsFilter `!@slow`)
- AC: `npm run typecheck` exits 0 and passes — the 3-arg `describe(name, { tags }, fn)` signature compiles under vitest 4.1.6
- AC: When a default `npm test` runs, it does not execute the UAT test 'delivers through real conversation routes and keeps plain forks off Channels MCP' — the test name is absent from the default-run output

title: pan-1824: Declare the @slow tag and exclude it from the default vitest run
pan-1821: Add a pan doctor warn row for rate-limited / suspended trackers
description:
Add checkTrackerRateLimits() to doctor.ts following the getCachedIssueRowsForDoctor pattern (:226 — fresh `new CacheService()` reading the same SQLite DB). For github/linear/rally read getRateLimit + getSuspensionMs. Return status 'warn' (name 'Tracker Rate Limits') naming any tracker with getSuspensionMs > 0 (suspended, include reset time) or shouldBackoff true; otherwise 'ok' with message 'All trackers within rate limits'. Wrap in try/catch returning an 'ok'/skip result on DB error, mirroring existing checks. Wire it into the doctor check list next to the other tracker/cache checks.
- AC: Given the rate_limits row for 'linear' has remaining:0 and a future reset, checkTrackerRateLimits() returns status 'warn' with a message naming 'linear' and the reset time.
- AC: Given all trackers are within limits (or have no rate_limits row), checkTrackerRateLimits() returns status 'ok' with message 'All trackers within rate limits'.
- AC: `pan doctor` shows the 'Tracker Rate Limits' row in its output, and a CacheService/DB error produces a non-throwing ok/skip result (doctor never throws on it).
close_reason: Implemented checkTrackerRateLimits doctor check with warn/ok/skip behavior and unit tests; typecheck/lint pass.

title: pan-1821: Expose backoffMs/suspendedUntil/rateLimited in cache-status diagnostics
description:
In getDiagnostics (issue-data-service.ts:550-564), for each tracker add: backoffMs = cache.getBackoffMs(tracker, POLL_INTERVALS[tracker].default) (0 if no intervals entry); suspendMs = cache.getSuspensionMs(tracker); suspendedUntil = suspendMs > 0 ? new Date(Date.now()+suspendMs).toISOString() : null; rateLimited = suspendMs > 0. These flow through GET /api/cache-status (routes/misc.ts:1512-1515) unchanged.
- AC: getDiagnostics() returns, for each tracker, a numeric backoffMs, a suspendedUntil field (ISO string or null), and a boolean rateLimited.
- AC: Given 'linear' is exhausted (remaining:0, reset in future), the diagnostics entry returns rateLimited:true and a non-null suspendedUntil approximately equal to the reset time.
- AC: Given a tracker within limits, its diagnostics entry returns rateLimited:false and suspendedUntil:null while preserving the existing remaining/total/pollInterval/lastFetched/lastError/issueCount fields.
close_reason: getDiagnostics now returns backoffMs, suspendedUntil, and rateLimited per tracker while preserving existing fields. Added 3 unit tests. Typecheck/lint pass.

title: pan-1821: Honor getSuspensionMs in scheduleNext to suspend exhausted trackers
description:
In scheduleNext (issue-data-service.ts:570-598), after computing effectiveInterval, call this.cache.getSuspensionMs(tracker). When > 0, set the next-poll delay to Math.min(suspendMs, 3_600_000) (D4 ceiling), bypassing the intervals.max clamp, log a warn line, and store it on state.currentInterval. When 0, use the existing effectiveInterval unchanged (NFR-1: non-exhausted cadence untouched). The setTimeout callback body (poll dispatch + reschedule) is unchanged.
- AC: Given a tracker whose getSuspensionMs returns 120_000, scheduleNext arms its timer for ~120_000 ms instead of the base interval (asserted with fake timers + a spied setTimeout or by advancing time and observing the poll fires only after the suspension).
- AC: Given getSuspensionMs returns a value above 3_600_000, the armed delay is clamped to 3_600_000 (D4 ceiling).
- AC: Given getSuspensionMs returns 0, scheduleNext arms the timer at the existing effectiveInterval (clamped to [min,max]) — GitHub/Rally/normal-Linear cadence is unchanged (NFR-1).
- AC: After a suspension delay elapses and the window resets (remaining restored), the next scheduleNext call returns to the normal interval — polling resumes automatically with no manual intervention.
close_reason: scheduleNext now honors getSuspensionMs, clamps to 1 hour, bypasses intervals.max for suspension, normal cadence untouched. Added 4 fake-timer tests. Typecheck/lint pass.

title: pan-1821: Record quota exhaustion on Linear 429 / RATELIMITED before throwing
description:
In fetchLinearIssues, when response.status === 429 OR the GraphQL error indicates rate-limiting (message contains 'rate limit' or 'RATELIMITED', consistent with linear-client.ts:139), ensure 'linear' is recorded as exhausted via cache.updateRateLimit('linear', { remaining: 0, total, resetAt }) before the existing throw. If the rate-limit headers were absent (ac of linear-rl-headers recorded nothing), derive resetAt from the Retry-After header (now + seconds) or default to now + 3_600_000 ms. Preserve the existing thrown Error so pollLinear's catch still sets lastError.
- AC: Given a Linear response with HTTP status 429, fetchLinearIssues records updateRateLimit('linear', { remaining: 0, … }) and then throws (verified: getRateLimit('linear').remaining === 0 after the rejected call).
- AC: Given a 429 with no rate-limit headers but a Retry-After header of N seconds, the recorded resetAt equals now + N seconds (asserted with fake timers).
- AC: Given a GraphQL body whose error message contains 'RATELIMITED' (HTTP 200), fetchLinearIssues still records remaining:0 and throws, defaulting resetAt to now + 1 hour when no reset/Retry-After is present.
close_reason: Added recordLinearExhaustion, detect HTTP 429 and GraphQL RATELIMITED, derive resetAt from Retry-After or +1h default, added 4 unit tests, typecheck/lint pass.

title: pan-1821: Add getSuspensionMs(tracker) to CacheService
description:
Add a pure method getSuspensionMs(tracker: string, now: number = Date.now()): number to CacheService (cache-service.ts). It reads getRateLimit(tracker); returns 0 when there is no limit row, when remaining > 0, when resetAt is unparseable, or when resetAt <= now; otherwise returns (resetMs - now). The `now` parameter makes it deterministically testable with fake timers. No schema change (NFR-3) — derived entirely from the existing rate_limits row. Callers are responsible for the 1-hour ceiling clamp (D4).
- AC: getSuspensionMs returns 0 when getRateLimit(tracker) is null, and 0 when remaining > 0 regardless of resetAt.
- AC: Given remaining === 0 and resetAt one hour in the future, getSuspensionMs returns approximately 3_600_000 (within tolerance, asserted with a fixed `now`).
- AC: Given remaining === 0 but resetAt already in the past (or unparseable), getSuspensionMs returns 0 (window already reset — no suspension).
close_reason: Added CacheService.getSuspensionMs with tests; returns 0 unless remaining <= 0 and resetAt is in the future. Typecheck/lint pass.

title: pan-1821: Parse Linear rate-limit headers and feed updateRateLimit('linear', …)
description:
In fetchLinearIssues (issue-data-service.ts ~:1089-1102), after `await fetch(...)` resolves and BEFORE `response.json()` / the json.errors throw, parse Linear's rate-limit headers and call this.cache.updateRateLimit('linear', { remaining, total, resetAt }). Use header names x-ratelimit-requests-remaining / -limit / -reset, falling back to the bare x-ratelimit-* names if absent. Normalize the reset value defensively (D2): a value < 1e12 is Unix seconds (×1000), otherwise Unix ms. Extract/reuse a parseIntegerHeader(headers, name) helper (see substrate-bug-poller.ts:105-110) that returns null on missing/unparseable values; skip the update (never throw) when remaining or total is null. Mirror the GitHub callback at :854-862.
- AC: Given a Linear fetch response carrying x-ratelimit-requests-remaining/-limit/-reset, fetchLinearIssues calls cache.updateRateLimit('linear', …) so getRateLimit('linear') returns the parsed remaining/total/resetAt (verified by a unit test with a mocked fetch).
- AC: Given a reset header value in Unix seconds (~1.7e9) and one in Unix milliseconds (~1.7e12), normalization produces the same correct future ISO-8601 resetAt (values <1e12 treated as seconds).
- AC: When the rate-limit headers are missing or unparseable, fetchLinearIssues does NOT call updateRateLimit and does NOT throw from the header-parsing step — issue fetching proceeds unchanged.
- AC: Header parsing runs before the json.errors throw, so a response that both carries rate-limit headers and a GraphQL error still records the rate limit (verified by a test where json.errors is set).
close_reason: Implemented Linear rate-limit header parsing in fetchLinearIssues, extracted parseIntegerHeader to cache-service.ts, added 7 unit tests, typecheck/lint pass.

title: pan-1827: Add pi branch + strike-/inspect- prefixes to the /messages agent fallback (conversations.ts)
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, in the GET /api/conversations/:name/messages route's no-conversation fallback (lines 3009-3066): (1) extend the prefix guard regex on line 3013 from /^(specialist-|agent-|planning-)/ to /^(specialist-|agent-|planning-|strike-|inspect-)/ so strike and inspect agents reach harness resolution. (2) Add a pi branch mirroring the codex branch (lines 3017-3024): immediately after the codex block, add `if (!sessionFile && await resolveAgentHarness(name) === 'pi') { const piPath = await resolvePiSessionPath(name); if (piPath) { sessionFile = piPath; setSpecialistSessionCache(name, piPath); } }`. Add resolvePiSessionPath to the existing import from './jsonl-resolver.js' (line 27). The resolved pi path is parsed by the existing getCachedMessages dispatch (line 3078 -> isPiSessionFile -> parsePiConversationMessages), so no parser change is needed. Leave the codex branch and the claude-projects scan intact and in order.
- AC: When name is a pipeline agent not in the conversations DB and resolveAgentHarness(name) === 'pi', the /messages route resolves sessionFile via resolvePiSessionPath(name) and caches it via setSpecialistSessionCache
- AC: The fallback prefix guard accepts strike- and inspect- session names (e.g. strike-pan-1723), so strike agents reach harness resolution
- AC: getCachedMessages routes a resolved pi session file to parsePiConversationMessages (via isPiSessionFile), so the /messages response returns a populated messages array for a pi work/strike agent
- AC: The fallback preserves the existing codex branch and the claude-projects scan, in the same order (codex checked first, then pi, then claude scan) — no regression for codex or claude-code agents
close_reason: Updated /api/conversations/:name/messages fallback to dispatch pi agents via resolvePiSessionPath and extended the agent prefix guard to include strike-/inspect- prefixes; conversations tests pass.

title: pan-1827: Regression tests for pi resolution in jsonl-resolver.test.ts (mirror codex cases)
description:
Add a new describe('resolveJsonlPath — pi agents (PAN-1827)') block to src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/jsonl-resolver.test.ts mirroring the existing codex describe (line 206). Build a pi agent fixture under agentsDir: state.json with harness:'pi', and a sessions/ subtree containing one or more .jsonl files with staggered mtimes via utimes (reuse the codex block's staggering pattern so 'freshest' is deterministic). Cover: resolves the freshest pi session JSONL; returns null when sessions/ is empty / absent; pi harness wins over a stale claude session.id + matching ~/.claude/projects JSONL; resolvePiSessionPath returns null for an agent without a sessions/ dir. Use real fs/promises temp dirs like the existing tests (no fake timers needed — these are not delay/retry tests).
- AC: A test asserts resolveJsonlPath returns the newest-mtime pi session .jsonl for a pi-harness agent fixture
- AC: A test asserts resolveJsonlPath returns null for a pi agent whose sessions/ directory has no .jsonl yet
- AC: A test asserts the pi harness wins over a stale claude session.id (mirrors the codex 'harness wins over stale claude session.id' case): with both a pi sessions JSONL and a ~/.claude/projects JSONL present, resolveJsonlPath returns the pi path
- AC: A test asserts resolvePiSessionPath returns null for an agent that has no sessions/ directory
close_reason: Added regression tests for resolvePiSessionPath and pi branch in jsonl-resolver.test.ts; 28 resolver tests pass.

title: pan-1827: Add resolvePiSessionPath + pi branch to resolveJsonlPath (jsonl-resolver.ts)
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/jsonl-resolver.ts add an exported async function resolvePiSessionPath(agentId, opts: ResolveJsonlPathOptions = {}) that mirrors resolveCodexRolloutPath (line 178): compute agentDir = <agentsDirOverride ?? ~/.panopticon/agents>/<agentId>, return null if <agentDir>/sessions does not exist, otherwise walk that tree with fs/promises (e.g. readdir(..., {recursive:true}) + stat), collect every *.jsonl, and return the absolute path with the newest mtime, or null when none exist. Then in resolveJsonlPath (line 204) add, immediately after the existing codex dispatch (line 211) and BEFORE the resolveClaudeSessionId call (line 215), a branch: if (await resolveAgentHarness(agentId, opts) === 'pi') return resolvePiSessionPath(agentId, opts). Combine the two harness reads into one resolveAgentHarness call to avoid a double read. Update the file-level docstring (lines 1-24) to mention the pi branch alongside codex. Use only fs/promises (no *Sync, no execSync). Do NOT import PiRuntimeSync.
- AC: resolvePiSessionPath('agent-x', {agentsDirOverride}) returns the freshest .jsonl found under <agentsDir>/agent-x/sessions/ (newest mtime), at any nesting depth
- AC: resolvePiSessionPath returns null when the agent has no sessions/ directory or it contains no .jsonl file
- AC: resolveJsonlPath returns the pi session path when the agent state.json harness is 'pi', dispatching the pi branch before the claude-code lookup
- AC: For a pi agent that also has a session.id file (a pi session id, not a claude UUID), resolveJsonlPath returns the pi sessions path and does NOT return a ~/.claude/projects/... path — the pi branch wins over the claude fallback
- AC: resolveJsonlPath still returns the codex rollout for codex agents and the claude JSONL for claude-code agents (existing dispatch preserved)
close_reason: Added resolvePiSessionPath and pi branch to resolveJsonlPath in jsonl-resolver.ts; existing resolver tests pass.

title: pan-1817: Add a tracker-quota row to `pan doctor`
description:
In src/cli/commands/doctor.ts add an exported checkTrackerQuota(): CheckResult[] (mirroring checkClosedIssueOrphanAgentDirs at 238 and getCachedIssueRowsForDoctor at 226: construct `new CacheService()` and read getPollHealth(tracker) for github/linear/rally plus getRateLimit('github')). Return 'warn' (or 'error') with a fix hint when a tracker reports quota_exhausted or GitHub remaining is at/near zero, and 'ok' otherwise. Wire it into doctorCommand alongside the other checks.push(...) calls (~723-727). Treat stale records as no-signal, consistent with the dashboard bead.
- AC: pan doctor renders a non-OK (warn/error) row naming the Linear quota with a fix hint when linear poll health is quota_exhausted
- AC: pan doctor renders a non-OK row naming GitHub quota when rate_limits remaining is at/near zero
- AC: pan doctor renders an OK tracker-quota row when no tracker reports a quota signal
- AC: doctorCommand emits the checkTrackerQuota result into its printed checks list (the check is wired in, not dead code)
close_reason: Added checkTrackerQuota to doctor.ts, wired into doctorCommand, added unit tests

title: pan-1817: Raise /api/system/health severity + reason on tracker-quota exhaustion
description:
In src/dashboard/server/services/system-health-service.ts, read the persisted poll health (cache.getPollHealth('linear'|'github'|'rally')) and GitHub remaining (cache.getRateLimit('github')) inside refreshSystemHealth (~583), treating any record older than a staleness window (e.g. 2× the poll interval) as no-signal. Pass a trackerQuota summary into evaluateSeverity (443-491) so a quota_exhausted tracker — or GitHub remaining at/near zero — appends a warning reason like 'Linear API quota exhausted — poller is rate-limited; lifecycle tracker steps may fail.'. Add a trackerQuota field to SystemHealthSnapshot in both system-health-service.ts (interface ~102) and src/dashboard/frontend/src/types.ts (~330). No new React component — SystemHealthPill (renders severity + reasons[0]) and HealthDashboard already display the reason.
- AC: A recent linear quota_exhausted record raises /api/system/health severity to at least 'warning' and produces a reasons[] entry naming Linear quota exhaustion
- AC: GitHub rate_limits remaining at zero raises severity to at least 'warning' and produces a reasons[] entry naming GitHub quota
- AC: With all trackers OK or no signal, refreshSystemHealth produces no tracker-quota reason and tracker state does not raise severity
- AC: A quota_exhausted record older than the staleness window preserves normal severity — the signal is not sticky (FR-4)
close_reason: Added checkTrackerQuota to system-health-service, trackerQuota field to SystemHealthSnapshot, wired quota reasons into evaluateSeverity, and added tests

title: pan-1817: Record poll outcomes (incl. rate-limit classification) from IssueDataService
description:
In src/dashboard/server/services/issue-data-service.ts add a private helper recordPollOutcome(tracker, error?) that classifies the error (case-insensitive /rate.?limit/i on err.message => 'quota_exhausted'; other error => 'error'; no error => 'ok') and calls this.cache.recordPollHealth(tracker, …). Call it in the success path and catch block of each poll method: pollLinear (success ~995, catch 1004-1007), pollGitHub (success 809, per-repo catch 797-799), pollRally (success 1353, catch 1362-1367). Because polling only runs on the host poller (peers run with skipPolling and never invoke these methods), peers record nothing. Do NOT call updateRateLimit('linear', …) — the rate_limits table stays GitHub-only here (NFR-1).
- AC: A Linear poll error whose message matches /rate.?limit/i persists poll health status 'quota_exhausted' for linear
- AC: A non-rate-limit Linear poll error persists poll health status 'error' for linear
- AC: A successful Linear poll persists poll health status 'ok', clearing any prior quota_exhausted signal (FR-4)
- AC: start({ skipPolling: true }) persists no poll health for any tracker — peers never poll (NFR-2)
- AC: pollLinear preserves the rate_limits table unchanged for linear — updateRateLimit is not called (NFR-1)
close_reason: Added recordPollOutcome helper to IssueDataService, classified rate-limit errors as quota_exhausted, wired success/catch calls in all three poll methods, and added tests

title: pan-1817: Add the missing idle-stack-reaper unit test (regression hardening)
description:
Create src/lib/cloister/__tests__/idle-stack-reaper.test.ts exercising reconcileIdleWorkspaceStacks (src/lib/cloister/idle-stack-reaper.ts) via injected IdleStackReaperDeps + __resetIdleStackReaperState() between cases. The reaper currently ships the test-only reset hook but has NO test file. Cover: (1) idle < graceMs => not reaped; (2) idle >= graceMs => server+frontend stop issued; (3) running agent / live tmux => never reaped; (4) grace clock resets when UI containers disappear; (5) PANOPTICON_DISABLE_STACK_REAPER short-circuits the patrol. Use injected deps/clock — do not rely on real wall-clock timers.
- AC: reconcileIdleWorkspaceStacks preserves a workspace idle less than graceMs (no container stop issued)
- AC: reconcileIdleWorkspaceStacks closes (stops) the server+frontend containers of a workspace idle longer than graceMs
- AC: reconcileIdleWorkspaceStacks preserves a workspace with a running agent or live tmux regardless of elapsed idle time
- AC: The grace clock resets when a workspace's UI containers disappear, and PANOPTICON_DISABLE_STACK_REAPER causes reconcileIdleWorkspaceStacks to exit without reaping
close_reason: Added idle-stack-reaper unit tests covering grace window, reaping, tmux protection, clock reset, and disable flag

title: pan-1817: Add a persisted per-tracker poll-health store to CacheService
description:
In src/dashboard/server/services/cache-service.ts add a new `tracker_poll_health` table (columns: tracker TEXT PRIMARY KEY, status TEXT, message TEXT, observed_at TEXT) inside createSchema() alongside the existing rate_limits table (around cache-service.ts:80). Add `recordPollHealth(tracker, { status, message })` (INSERT … ON CONFLICT(tracker) DO UPDATE — upsert, stamping observed_at) and `getPollHealth(tracker): PollHealth | null` mirroring the existing updateRateLimit/getRateLimit pair (cache-service.ts:289-322). Export a PollHealthStatus = 'ok' | 'quota_exhausted' | 'error' type and a PollHealth interface { status, message, observedAt }. This is the foundation API the recording, dashboard-health, and doctor beads all consume — keep the method signatures stable.
- AC: recordPollHealth persists a tracker_poll_health row that getPollHealth returns with the same status and message
- AC: getPollHealth returns null for a tracker with no recorded poll outcome
- AC: A second recordPollHealth for the same tracker persists (upserts) the latest status, and getPollHealth returns only the newest row
- AC: createSchema creates tracker_poll_health idempotently (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) and preserves the existing rate_limits schema unchanged
close_reason: Added tracker_poll_health table, recordPollHealth/getPollHealth APIs, PollHealth/PollHealthStatus types, and unit tests in CacheService

title: pan-1802: Truncate (head+tail) and retry once when a summary chunk overflows
description:
In src/lib/conversations/smart-compaction.ts, make generateSummaryFromPrompt (:791) resilient to context overflow. Add a head+tail truncation helper (preserve the first and last portions, insert an elision marker like '[... N characters truncated ...]'). When the underlying runModelSummary call rejects AND isContextOverflowError(err) is true (from the overflow-detector bead), rebuild the prompt with the serialized chunk truncated head+tail to a per-model ceiling derived from getChunkBudgetChars(model), also capping previousSummary, and retry exactly once. If the retried call also overflows (or any non-overflow error occurs), reject so the caller's fallback handles it. Do not loop. This single chokepoint covers all generateSummaryFromPrompt call sites (chunked loops and non-chunked) and authorHandoffExternal precompaction.
- AC: Given the first runModelSummary call rejects with a context-overflow error, generateSummaryFromPrompt truncates the serialized chunk head+tail and retries once, returning the degraded summary from the second (truncated) call instead of throwing.
- AC: The head+tail truncation helper, given input longer than the ceiling, returns text containing both the original head and the original tail plus an elision marker, and is no longer than the ceiling plus the marker; verified by a unit test on a long string.
- AC: When the retried (truncated) call also rejects with overflow, generateSummaryFromPrompt rejects (no third attempt, no infinite loop), surfacing the error to its caller.
- AC: When the first runModelSummary call succeeds, generateSummaryFromPrompt returns that result unchanged and makes no second call (asserted by call count), so normal-sized summaries are unaffected.
close_reason: Added truncateHeadTail helper and one-retry logic to generateSummaryFromPrompt; capping serialized chunk and previousSummary to getChunkBudgetChars(model) on context overflow. Covered by unit tests.

title: pan-1802: Document focus limit and large-source auto-degrade in --help and skill
description:
Update the handoff command help in src/cli/index.ts (:433-441) so --help states that very large source conversations are auto-degraded (smart summary truncated, then heuristic/focus-only) and STILL hand off without aborting (the 500-char focus limit is already mentioned at :434). Update sync-sources/skills/pan-handoff/SKILL.md 'Fallback behavior' section (:90-99) to list the handoff-request-failed / degraded-summary fallback reason and to state that an oversized source produces a degraded handoff rather than a failure. Both files MUST change in the same commit (skills↔CLI lockstep) so scripts/lint-skills.sh stays green.
- AC: Running `pan handoff --help` prints text stating the focus 500-character limit AND that very large source conversations are auto-degraded and still hand off without aborting.
- AC: The pan-handoff SKILL.md 'Fallback behavior' section contains the handoff-request-failed fallback reason and the statement that an oversized source produces a degraded handoff, not a failure.

title: pan-1802: Mark the conversation ended when a fork pipeline fails terminally
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, the fork route's pipeline error handler (:4288-4291) sets forkStatus='failed' but leaves the conversation status='active' (set at :4284), producing a live ghost. Add markConversationEnded(newConv.name) inside that .catch alongside updateForkStatus(newConv.name,'failed',msg) so a fork that dies before spawn is surfaced as ended, not active. Do not delete the conversation.
- AC: When runForkPipeline rejects, the fork route's catch calls markConversationEnded(name) in addition to updateForkStatus(name,'failed',msg).
- AC: When the fork pipeline rejects, the conversation row persists status 'ended' and forkStatus 'failed' (not status 'active'), verified by a test that forces the pipeline to reject.
close_reason: Extracted handleForkPipelineFailure so the fork route catch marks the conversation ended on terminal pipeline failure, preventing ghost active rows. Added unit tests.

title: pan-1802: Guard the handoff fallback summary so the pipeline always spawns
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts runForkPipeline (:3862), the handoff fallback paths (catch blocks at :3948-3953, :3956-3961, :3963-3972) call buildSummary() unguarded — a throw escapes the pipeline. Wrap the fallback so a throw drops to generateFallbackSummary(parentSessionFile) (heuristic, no LLM, cannot overflow), and a further throw drops to an empty summary string; prependFallbackFocus(...) still preserves the operator focus. After the handoff block, the pipeline must continue to updateForkStatus('spawning') + ensureForkSessionReady + injectForkSummary + markConversationActive + updateForkStatus(null) regardless of which fallback tier produced the summary. forkFallbackReason continues to be persisted via updateConversationForkFallbackReason.
- AC: Given authorHandoffExternal throws AND the LLM fallback summary throws a context-overflow error, runForkPipeline seeds the successor from generateFallbackSummary and resolves successfully — ensureForkSessionReady is invoked and forkStatus is cleared to null (the pipeline does not reject).
- AC: Given the heuristic generateFallbackSummary ALSO throws, runForkPipeline spawns with a focus-only seed (empty summary passed through prependFallbackFocus) and still clears forkStatus to null without rejecting.
- AC: After a fallback, the successor's forkFallbackReason is persisted (updateConversationForkFallbackReason called with the reason) so the CLI prints the yellow 'Handoff fell back to summary fork' notice.
- AC: A test mocking authorHandoffExternal and generateSummaryForFork to throw overflow asserts runForkPipeline invokes ensureForkSessionReady and resolves without rejecting.
close_reason: Hardened runForkPipeline fallback summary path to degrade from LLM summary to heuristic fallback to empty summary, always reaching ensureForkSessionReady. Added unit tests.

title: pan-1802: Add a claude -p context-overflow detector to context-overflow.ts
description:
Extend src/lib/context-overflow.ts with a pure matcher isContextOverflowError(err: unknown): boolean that recognizes the summarizer overflow signature. Add the claude -p phrasings ('prompt is too long', 'blocking_limit') to a patterns list and match case-insensitively against the error message (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)), also reusing the existing CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_PATTERNS so the 400 'input exceeds the context window' tail is covered. No I/O, no side effects.
- AC: isContextOverflowError returns true for an Error whose message contains the claude -p blocking-limit envelope (e.g. '..."result":"Prompt is too long"..."terminal_reason":"blocking_limit"...') and matches case-insensitively.
- AC: isContextOverflowError returns true for the existing 'input exceeds the context window' phrasing and returns false for an unrelated error such as 'ENOENT: no such file'.
- AC: src/lib/context-overflow.ts provides a named isContextOverflowError(err) helper that returns a boolean and is importable by the summarizer module.
close_reason: Added isContextOverflowError to src/lib/context-overflow.ts with unit tests covering claude -p blocking_limit and existing context-window patterns

title: pan-1818: Reconcile gpt-5.5 capability notes with its 150k CLIProxy ceiling and lock with a test
description:
In src/lib/model-capabilities.ts gpt-5.5 entry (:460-481): replace the contradictory notes clause 'Effective Claude Code/CLIProxy ceiling is 200K, 128K max output.' with text consistent with the 150k conservative ceiling, e.g. 'Routed via CLIProxy; conservative effective context ceiling 150K (CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW, PAN-1672), 128K max output.' Do NOT change contextWindow (already CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW) or any skill score. Add a test asserting MODEL_CAPABILITIES['gpt-5.5'].contextWindow === CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW (new model-capabilities unit test, or extend conversation-service.test.ts which already references the 150k window at :111).
- AC: Given the gpt-5.5 capability entry, then its notes no longer claim a 200K effective ceiling and instead state the 150K conservative CLIProxy ceiling.
- AC: A unit test preserves the invariant that MODEL_CAPABILITIES['gpt-5.5'].contextWindow equals CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW, failing if the two ever diverge.
- AC: Given the change, then MODEL_CAPABILITIES['gpt-5.5'].contextWindow still equals 150000 and no gpt-5.5 skill score is modified (notes-only edit, value preserved).

title: pan-1818: Add large-changeset signal to review manifest + conditional selective-reading guardrail in Tier-1 summary
description:
In src/lib/cloister/review-context.ts: add module constants REVIEW_LARGE_CHANGESET_FILES=25 and REVIEW_LARGE_CHANGESET_LINES=1500; add a largeChangeset field { fileCount: number; changedLines: number; isLarge: boolean } to ReviewContextManifest (:67) computed in the manifest build (fileCount=changedFiles.length, changedLines=sum of additions+deletions, isLarge=fileCount>FILES || changedLines>LINES). In formatTier1Summary() (~:331), when manifest.largeChangeset.isLarge, append a guidance block instructing the reviewer to read the highest-risk files first, read only changed regions (not whole files), and if approaching the context limit write a report on what was reviewed and flag the uncovered high-risk files as a BLOCKING coverage gap rather than reading everything and overflowing. When not large, append nothing.
- AC: Given a changeset of >25 files or >1500 total changed lines, when buildReviewContext runs, then manifest.largeChangeset.isLarge === true with correct fileCount and changedLines.
- AC: Given a large changeset manifest, when formatTier1Summary runs, then its output contains the selective-reading guardrail block (mentions reading highest-risk files first and flagging uncovered files as a blocking coverage gap).
- AC: Given a small changeset (<=25 files and <=1500 lines), when formatTier1Summary runs, then isLarge === false and the guardrail block is absent (small-PR prompts unchanged).

title: pan-1818: Exclude convoy reviewer sub-role sessions from checkApiErrorAgents generic recovery
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents() (~:964), skip sessions matching the convoy reviewer pattern /^agent-.*-review-(?:security|correctness|performance|requirements)$/ (the same regex used by cleanupOrphanedReviewSessions at :5957) — either by filtering them out of agentSessions (:977-979) or continuing at the top of the per-session loop. Add a one-line comment (PAN-1818) that monitorReviewConvoySignals() is the sole lifecycle owner of these sessions. This prevents the garbage-issueId work-agent recovery (resumeAgent compact-respawn / markWorkspaceStuck) from acting on reviewers and racing the monitor. Do NOT change handling of the synthesis session (agent-<issue>-review) or any work/planning session.
- AC: Given a session named agent-pan-1815-review-correctness whose pane tail shows a context-overflow 400, when checkApiErrorAgents runs, then it performs NO recovery action on it: resumeAgent, markWorkspaceStuck, and sendKeys are never called for that session.
- AC: Given a normal work-agent session agent-pan-1815 with a context-overflow 400, when checkApiErrorAgents runs, then its existing recovery still fires (regression guard that the exclusion is scoped to reviewer sub-role sessions only).

title: pan-1818: Fast-fail convoy reviewers on context overflow in monitorReviewConvoySignals (no respawn)
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts monitorReviewConvoySignals() (~:5788), after outputWrittenForThisRun and reviewerSessionAlive are computed and BEFORE the existing signal if/else ladder (~:5862), add an overflow check: when !outputWrittenForThisRun, capture the reviewer pane tail via await Effect.runPromise(capturePane(agentId, 100)) (already imported at :156) and test with isContextOverflowTail() (import from '../context-overflow.js'). On match, set signal='failed', reason='context-window overflow (no retry — deterministic)', reaching this branch before the idleAndNoOutput/respawnIdleReviewer branch (~:5868) so an overflowed reviewer never respawns. Leave REVIEWER_FAILED message construction and reviewMonitorSignaled write unchanged.
- AC: Given a convoy reviewer that is alive, has written no output for this run, and whose pane tail contains 'input exceeds the context window', when monitorReviewConvoySignals runs, then it sends the synthesis agent REVIEWER_FAILED <subRole> with a reason naming 'context-window overflow'.
- AC: Given the same overflowed reviewer, when monitorReviewConvoySignals runs, then respawnIdleReviewer is NOT invoked (no respawn for deterministic overflow).
- AC: Given a reviewer that is idle with no output but whose tail shows NO overflow pattern, when monitorReviewConvoySignals runs, then the existing PAN-1806 idle-respawn path is preserved (respawnIdleReviewer still invoked on first idle detection).
- AC: Given an overflowed reviewer, the monitor emits the REVIEWER_FAILED signal on the first pass that observes the overflow tail, without waiting for the 3-minute REVIEWER_IDLE_FAILURE_MS idle threshold.

title: pan-1834: Introduce 'rateLimit' PendingInputKind and promote 'rate_limit' detections into pendingInputKinds
description:
Add 'rateLimit' to the PendingInputKind union in BOTH src/lib/agent-enrichment.ts (line 45) and src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/pendingInput.ts (line 12), and add a label to PENDING_INPUT_KIND_LABEL in pendingInput.ts (e.g. 'Rate-limit modal — pick a model'). In computeAgentEnrichmentPromise (agent-enrichment.ts), promote a rate_limit detection into pendingInputKinds, mirroring the session_resume promotion at line 502.

- AC: PENDING_INPUT_KIND_LABEL contains a human label for 'rateLimit'

title: pan-1834: Document the rate-limit kind, specialist-unsuppression, inspect enumeration, and activity/TTS notification
description:
Update docs/ASKUSERQUESTION-DASHBOARD.md (the awaiting-input field guide) to cover the new rateLimit kind + rate-limit modal pane signature, the hasActiveSpecialist unsuppression for active specialists, inspect-agent enumeration via a minimal state.json, and the rising-edge activity-feed + TTS notification.

title: pan-1834: Emit an activity-feed entry + TTS on the rising edge of an agent becoming blocked on input
description:
In src/dashboard/server/services/agent-enrichment-service.ts pollOnce, after computing enrichment and before/with the enrichment_changed emit, detect the rising edge: previous enrichment pendingInputCount === 0 (or absent) AND new enrichment pendingInputCount > 0. On that edge call emitActivityEntrySync (source = the agent role, level 'warn', message naming the agent, issueId, and the pending kind(s) via describePendingInput) and emitActivityTtsSync (priority 1, issueId, a short speakable utterance). Extract a pure helper for the edge decision + message text so it is unit-testable. Do not emit while the agent stays blocked across subsequent polls.
- AC: When an agent's pendingInputCount rises from 0 to greater than 0 across polls, the poller emits one activity.entry naming the agent, its issueId, and the pending kind(s)
- AC: The same rising edge emits one activity.tts with priority 1 naming the blocked agent and what it is waiting for
- AC: While the agent stays blocked across subsequent polls, the poller emits no further activity.entry or activity.tts for the same block
- AC: The emitted activity.entry contains the agent's issueId so the activity feed associates it with the issue
close_reason: Added isAwaitingInputRisingEdge and buildAwaitingInputActivityMessage helpers and integrated activity.entry + activity.tts emission into agent-enrichment-service pollOnce on the rising edge of pendingInputCount.

title: pan-1834: Make inspect agents enumerable by writing a minimal state.json at spawn
description:
In src/lib/cloister/inspect-agent.ts, after createSession, write a minimal AgentState via saveAgentState for the inspect agent id (tmuxSession) carrying issueId=context.issueId, workspace=context.workspace, role='work', and the inspect subRole, mirroring how review-agent.ts:281 persists sub-reviewer state. This makes listRunningAgents (which requires a readable state.json via getAgentStateSync) enumerate inspect agents so the enrichment poller scans them. Do NOT change deacon patrols — they filter on agent-/specialist-/planning- prefixes and already exclude inspect-.
- AC: The inspect spawn path saves a state.json for the inspect agent carrying its workspace, issueId, and role
- AC: getAgentStateSync('inspect-<issue>-<bead>') returns a non-null AgentState after an inspect agent spawns
- AC: listRunningAgents contains the inspect agent with tmuxActive reflecting its live tmux session
- AC: The inspect agent id keeps the 'inspect-' prefix so deacon orphan-recovery refuses to auto-resume it

title: pan-1834: Do not suppress pending-input for an agent that is itself the active specialist
description:
In src/lib/agent-enrichment.ts computeAgentEnrichmentPromise, add a pure helper that decides whether the agent is its own active specialist (derived role in {review, test, ship}). Replace the two suppression checks (hasPendingQuestion at line 475 and the if (!hasActiveSpecialist) block at line 483) so suppression applies only when hasActiveSpecialist is true AND the agent is NOT its own active specialist. This corrects all three call sites (poller, read-model.ts:674, routes/agents.ts:765) at once. Add unit coverage for the helper.
- AC: A pure helper returns true when the agent's derived role is review, test, or ship, and returns false for work, plan, or undefined
- AC: computeAgentEnrichmentPromise produces a non-empty pendingInputKinds for a review-role agent when hasActiveSpecialist is true and a detection fires
- AC: computeAgentEnrichmentPromise produces an empty pendingInputKinds for a work-role agent when hasActiveSpecialist is true, preserving existing parked-agent suppression
- AC: hasPendingQuestion returns true for a review-role agent with a detection even when hasActiveSpecialist is true
close_reason: Replaced blanket hasActiveSpecialist suppression with shouldSuppressPendingInput = hasActiveSpecialist && !isOwnActiveSpecialist(role); added helper and unit tests including mocked computeAgentEnrichment cases.

title: pan-1834: Add 'rate_limit' pane signature for the harness rate-limit/model-switch modal
description:
In src/lib/agent-input-detection.ts, add 'rate_limit' to the AwaitingInputReason union (line 5) and a new detection branch in detectAwaitingInputFromPaneSync (after the session-resume branch, ~line 224). Add a looksLikeRateLimitModelSwitchModal(text) helper that requires a 'Keep current model' option line AND a 'Switch to <model>' option line. Gate on isRecentPromptIndex so an answered/scrolled-away modal does not re-fire. Add positive and negative cases to src/lib/__tests__/agent-input-detection.test.ts.

title: pan-1849: Add a Step-0 main-health gate to the orchestrator prompt
description:
Add a 'Main-health gate' step to roles/flywheel.md ahead of the tick loop: each tick call GET /api/flywheel/main-health; when status is 'red' the top-ranked suggestion MUST be a main-fix (file a substrate bug capturing reason/failingGates if none exists, then launch it via pan strike or pan plan --auto --auto-start), and no new feature/substrate work may be launched until main is green (finishing in-flight work that fixes main is allowed). Keep the orchestrator's no-direct-code-edits posture.
- AC: roles/flywheel.md contains a 'Main-health gate' step that directs reading GET /api/flywheel/main-health before ranking suggestions each tick.
- AC: roles/flywheel.md contains the rule that a red main makes a main-fix the top-ranked suggestion and blocks new feature/substrate launches until green.
- AC: roles/flywheel.md contains instructions to file a substrate bug from reason/failingGates and launch the fix via the normal pipeline (no direct code edits).

title: pan-1849: Surface a main-health badge on the Flywheel page
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx, poll GET /api/flywheel/main-health (mirroring the existing 5s status poll) and render a red/green/unknown badge in the header near the run badge (:429), plus a reason + failing-gates line when red. Follow pan-style-guide: danger token for red, success token for green, muted token for unknown; no pill badge, no decorative color. The badge must render from the endpoint even when no flywheel run is active.
- AC: When the endpoint returns status 'red', the page renders a danger-toned main-health badge that shows the reason text.
- AC: When status is 'green' the page renders a success-toned badge; when 'unknown' it renders a muted badge.
- AC: Given a null active run and red main-health, a component test shows the red badge still renders (orchestrator-independent).

title: pan-1849: Feed the browser smoke into main-health detection
description:
In checkMainHealth (src/lib/cloister/main-health.ts) run the browser smoke script via execAsync after the test gate, parse its JSON result, and fold it into the main-health state: smoke 'fail' forces status 'red' even when the test gate passed; smoke 'unavailable' records smoke:'unavailable' and never flips a green test result to red; smoke 'pass' keeps the test-gate verdict. Store the smoke sub-result on the persisted FlywheelMainHealth.smoke field.
- AC: When the browser smoke reports 'fail', checkMainHealth resolves status 'red' even if the test gate passed.
- AC: When the smoke reports 'unavailable', checkMainHealth records smoke 'unavailable' and does not flip a green test result to red.
- AC: checkMainHealth persists the smoke sub-result so GET /api/flywheel/main-health returns the smoke field.

title: pan-1849: pan flywheel main-health [--check] CLI + skill
description:
Add a pan flywheel main-health subcommand in src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts (the command block at :910) that prints the current main-health state (status, reason, failingGates) read from GET /api/flywheel/main-health or getMainHealth() directly; with --check it runs checkMainHealth({ source: 'cli' }), persists, and prints the fresh result. Update skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md to document the new verb and the --check flag in the same commit so scripts/lint-skills.sh passes.

- AC: pan flywheel main-health --check runs detection, updates the persisted state, and prints the fresh result.
- AC: npm run lint passes — skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md documents main-health and --check matching pan flywheel --help.

title: pan-1849: Re-check main-health after each merge (idempotent fire-and-forget)
description:
In postMergeLifecycle() (src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts) at the late insertion point (~:566, after notifyTldrDaemon and before the _completedPostMerge marker), trigger checkMainHealth({ source: 'post-merge' }) as a non-awaited fire-and-forget (void checkMainHealth(...).catch(log)) so it neither blocks the lifecycle nor re-enters it. Do not touch the in-flight guard or the completion-marker logic.
