- AC: When checkMainHealth rejects, postMergeLifecycle still resolves and still sets the completion marker.
- AC: The in-flight-guard idempotency test passes — postMergeLifecycle still runs at most once per merge.

title: pan-1849: Run checkMainHealth from the deacon patrol on a gated cadence
description:
Add main_health_every_cycles to MonitoringConfig (src/lib/cloister/config.ts:48) with a documented default in the defaults block (config.ts:306) and plumb it into the deacon config the same way stash_janitor_every_cycles is (deacon.ts:287). In runPatrol() (deacon.ts), after the existing gated checks, call checkMainHealth({ source: 'patrol' }) only when patrolCycle % mainHealthEveryCycles === 0, push a log action describing the result, and guard the call so a rejection cannot escape the patrol cycle. Use the existing every-N-cycles arithmetic pattern at deacon.ts:5179 as the template.
- AC: Given a config with main_health_every_cycles set, the deacon respects that value; when unset it defaults to the documented cadence.
- AC: When patrolCycle % mainHealthEveryCycles === 0 the patrol spawns the main-health check, and on other cycles it does not (gating arithmetic test).
- AC: When checkMainHealth rejects, runPatrol still resolves a PatrolResult (the patrol does not crash).

title: pan-1849: Deterministically rank a main-fix top when main is red
description:
Add a pure applyMainHealthPriority(snapshot, mainHealth) helper (new module, e.g. src/lib/flywheel-main-health-priority.ts) that stamps snapshot.mainHealth = mainHealth and, when mainHealth.status === 'red', prepends an urgent suggestion { action: 'investigate', rationale: 'main is red: <reason> (failing: <gates>)', priority: 'urgent' } to suggestions[] unless an equivalent main-fix already leads (dedupe). For green/unknown it leaves suggestions unchanged. Wire it into postFlywheelStatusPayload (src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts:196), reading getMainHealth(), before the snapshot is persisted.
- AC: Given red main-health and a snapshot whose first suggestion is a feature 'start', applyMainHealthPriority returns a snapshot whose first suggestion is the urgent main-fix 'investigate'.
- AC: Calling applyMainHealthPriority twice with red main-health produces only one injected main-fix suggestion (deduped).
- AC: Given green main-health, applyMainHealthPriority preserves the original suggestions and stamps snapshot.mainHealth.status to 'green'.
- AC: postFlywheelStatusPayload persists a snapshot whose mainHealth reflects the current getMainHealth() value.

title: pan-1849: GET /api/flywheel/main-health returns the persisted state
description:
Add getMainHealthPayload() in src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts returning getMainHealth(), or a default { status: 'unknown', lastCheckedAt: null } body when no check has run, and register a GET /api/flywheel/main-health route alongside the other flywheel GET routes (near :525).
- AC: GET /api/flywheel/main-health returns 200 with the persisted main-health state when one exists.
- AC: When no check has run yet, GET /api/flywheel/main-health returns 200 with a status 'unknown' body (never 404 or 500).

title: pan-1849: checkMainHealth(): run the test gate against origin/main and record state
description:
Create src/lib/cloister/main-health.ts exporting async checkMainHealth(opts?: { source?: 'patrol'|'post-merge'|'cli'; projectRoot?; now? }): Promise<FlywheelMainHealth>. Resolve the Panopticon project root and its configured 'test' quality gate (fall back to DEFAULT_GATES.test). Run git fetch origin main via execAsync; if the primary worktree is dirty or HEAD differs from origin/main, persist and return status 'unknown' with a reason WITHOUT running tests. Otherwise run the test gate via runQualityGates with the {{CHANGED_BASE}} placeholder set to the stored lastGreenSha (or the project default when none). Interpret: all required gates pass => 'green' and advance lastGreenSha/lastGreenAt to the checked SHA; any required gate fails => 'red' with failingGates and a short reason. Wrap the whole body so a thrown git/infra error resolves to 'unknown' (never a rejected promise). Persist via setMainHealth and return. Use execAsync/spawn only — never execSync.
- AC: Given a stubbed gate runner returning all-pass, checkMainHealth resolves status 'green' and sets lastGreenSha to the checked SHA.
- AC: Given a stubbed gate runner where the test gate fails, checkMainHealth resolves status 'red', failingGates contains 'test', and lastGreenSha is preserved (not advanced).
- AC: When the primary worktree is dirty or HEAD differs from origin/main, checkMainHealth resolves status 'unknown' and the gate runner is never spawned.
- AC: checkMainHealth persists its result via setMainHealth so a subsequent getMainHealth() returns the same status.
- AC: When the underlying git/fetch step throws, checkMainHealth resolves status 'unknown' and never rejects, so a caller patrol cannot crash on it.

title: pan-1849: Headless browser smoke script (home + Settings + a conversation)
description:
Create a standalone Node script (e.g. scripts/main-smoke.mjs) using the installed playwright package. Launch headless chromium with a fresh isolated browser context, navigate to the dashboard home (/), Settings, and a conversation page (/conv/<id> using the most recent id from GET /api/conversations, skipping that page gracefully when none exists), collecting pageerror and console.error events and checking for the React error-boundary fallback rendered by src/dashboard/frontend/src/recovery.tsx. Print a JSON summary { smoke: 'pass'|'fail'|'unavailable', failures: [...] } and exit 0 on pass, non-zero on fail, and a distinct unavailable code when playwright or the browser binary is missing. Close the context before exit.
- AC: Run against a healthy dashboard, the script exits 0 and prints a JSON summary with smoke 'pass'.
- AC: When a visited route logs an uncaught console error or renders the error-boundary fallback, the script exits non-zero and reports smoke 'fail' with the offending route.
- AC: The script opens an isolated Playwright browser context (fresh profile) and closes it before exiting.
- AC: When playwright or the browser binary is unavailable, the script exits with the unavailable code and prints smoke 'unavailable' (never a false fail).

title: pan-1849: Define FlywheelMainHealth contract + app_settings persistence
description:
Add an Effect Schema.Struct FlywheelMainHealth in packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts with fields status (Literals green|red|unknown), checkedSha?, lastGreenSha?, lastGreenAt?, lastCheckedAt, reason?, failingGates? (Array String), source (Literals patrol|post-merge|cli|smoke), and smoke? (Literals pass|fail|unavailable); export the interface + schema and re-export from the contracts index. Add mainHealth: Schema.optional(FlywheelMainHealth) to the FlywheelStatus struct and interface. In src/lib/database/app-settings.ts add FLYWHEEL_MAIN_HEALTH_KEY = 'flywheel.main_health' plus getMainHealth() (JSON.parse, null on missing/malformed) and setMainHealth(state) (JSON.stringify via setSetting). Rebuild packages/contracts.
- AC: Given a value with status 'green'|'red'|'unknown', the FlywheelMainHealth schema accepts it and rejects any other status string.
- AC: FlywheelStatus accepts a snapshot carrying mainHealth and still decodes a snapshot that omits it (back-compat preserved).
- AC: Given setMainHealth(x) was called, getMainHealth() returns the same object; getMainHealth() returns null when the key is absent or stores malformed JSON.
- AC: A persisted main-health state survives a fresh getDatabase() handle: getMainHealth() returns it after the DB is re-opened.

title: pan-1832: Settings → Roles distribution editor (add/remove models + weights)
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx widen the local types (RoleConfig.model?: ModelRef | Array<{model: ModelRef; weight: number}>; same for RoleSubConfig). Extend ModelPicker (:321) with a distribution mode: a toggle/button 'Split across models' that switches a row between the single <select> (today) and a distribution editor — N rows, each = the existing model <select> + an integer weight number input + a remove button, plus an 'Add model' button. Show the live effective % per row (weight / Σweights). getRoleModel/getSubRoleModel/saveRoleConfig (:169/:173/:271) must read and write the union and POST the list verbatim in the same /api/settings PUT shape settings-api validation expects (array of {model, weight}). Follow pan-style-guide (no pill badges, muted text, existing input classes). When a distribution has a single entry, saving may collapse it back to a scalar (optional).
- AC: Toggling a role to 'split' mode renders one row per model with a model <select>, an integer weight input, a remove button, and an 'Add model' button
- AC: Adding/removing rows and editing a weight updates the displayed effective % (weight/Σ) live without a page reload
- AC: Saving a distribution emits a PUT with roles.<role>.model as an array of {model, weight} matching the settings-api shape; the editor renders the saved value on refetch
- AC: A role still configured with a single model renders the existing single <select> (scalar UX unchanged) and saves a scalar ref

title: pan-1832: Seed inspect, plan, and flywheel model resolution
description:
Route the remaining pipeline spawn sites through the seeded resolver, each seeded by its own stable agent id. (1) src/lib/cloister/inspect-agent.ts (:176): resolveModel('work', subRole, config) → resolveModelForSpawn('work', subRole, <inspect agent id, e.g. tmuxSession>, config). (2) src/lib/planning/spawn-planning-session.ts (:556): resolveModel('plan', undefined, ...) → resolveModelForSpawn('plan', undefined, <plan agent id>, ...). (3) Flywheel (singleton): src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-actions.ts (:126) and src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts (:260): resolveModel('flywheel', ...) → resolveModelForSpawn('flywheel', undefined, <flywheel agent id>, config) — note in a code comment that flywheel is a singleton so a list resolves to a single deterministic pick, not a real split. Leave the STATIC/display callers (router-config.ts, routes/specialists.ts:1662, routes/misc.ts:1227 continuation launcher, specialist-context.ts:128 digest) on the deterministic resolveModel — do not reroute them.
- AC: inspect-agent.ts resolves its model via resolveModelForSpawn('work', subRole, <inspect agent id>, config); a list config yields a sampled deref'd model
- AC: spawn-planning-session.ts resolves the plan model via resolveModelForSpawn('plan', undefined, <plan agent id>, config)
- AC: Both flywheel callers (flywheel-actions.ts:126, cli/commands/flywheel.ts:260) use resolveModelForSpawn('flywheel', ...) and carry a comment that a singleton resolves a list to one deterministic pick
- AC: The static/display callers (router-config.ts, routes/specialists.ts:1662, routes/misc.ts:1227, specialist-context.ts:128) each preserves deterministic resolution — a list config resolves to the primary there, unchanged

title: pan-1832: Sample review-convoy models seeded by reviewer agent id
description:
In src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts (:236) replace `opts.model ?? resolveModel('review', opts.subRole, cfg)` with `opts.model ?? resolveModelForSpawn('review', opts.subRole, reviewerAgentId(opts.issueId, opts.subRole), cfg)` so each review sub-role (security/correctness/performance/requirements) samples by its own stable reviewer agent id. The explicit opts.model override path is unchanged.
- AC: Each review sub-role resolves its model via resolveModelForSpawn seeded by reviewerAgentId(issueId, subRole); a list config produces a deref'd ModelId from the list
- AC: Re-running the same issue + sub-role preserves the same sampled review model (idempotent); different sub-roles/issues produce different models to spread load
- AC: An explicit opts.model still wins over the distribution for the reviewer spawn

title: pan-1832: Seed determineModel and thread it through work/test/ship/strike spawns
description:
Give determineModel a seed and route every work-class spawn through the seeded resolver. In src/lib/agents.ts change determineModel({model, role}) → determineModel({model, role, seed}): when there is no explicit modelOverride, resolve via resolveModelForSpawn(role ?? 'work', undefined, seed ?? '', loadYamlConfig().config) (a missing seed deterministically yields the primary). Keep the WORK_AGENT_BROKEN_MODELS safety net. Thread the stable agent id as seed at: spawnAgent (:3469, agentId = `agent-<issue>` already computed at :3449); spawnRun (:3133 — reorder so the role-run agentId/`agent-<issue>` is computed before determineModel, then pass it); the dashboard start route routes/agents.ts (:2792, seed `agent-<issueId.toLowerCase()>`); and runtimes/claude-code.ts (:350, seed: config.agentId). After resolution, the EXISTING resolveHarness({explicit, role, model: selectedModel}) call must receive the sampled model so harness follows it. Do NOT make resolveModel sample.
- AC: Re-spawning the work agent for the same issue id preserves the same sampled model from a list config (idempotent); state.model returns unchanged on the second spawn
- AC: Across many issue ids, work spawns from a [70,30] list realize ≈70/30 (statistical test over generated agent ids)
- AC: An explicit per-spawn model override (options.model) still wins over the distribution; a seedless determineModel returns the primary (first) entry deterministically
- AC: The spawned agent's state.model records the sampled model and resolveHarness is called with that sampled model (harness follows the chosen model)
- AC: A spawn via routes/agents.ts:2792 and a spawn via runtimes/claude-code.ts:350 each produces a sampled, non-primary model across many issue ids, confirming both call sites thread a seed

title: pan-1832: Validate the list form + effort-vs-list in the Settings API
description:
In src/lib/settings-api.ts add validateRoleModelConfig(fieldPath, model, effectiveWorkhorses, errors, warnings, allowWorkhorseRef, allowParentRef): if model is a string, delegate to the existing validateModelRef; if it is an array, require non-empty, each entry an object with a string model and integer weight > 0, total > 0, reject 'parent' as a list entry, and validate each model via validateModelRef (workhorse refs allowed). Call it where validateModelRef is currently called for roles.<role>.model (:517) and roles.<role>.sub.<subRole>.model (:549). Extend the effort check (:521-533): when the role model is a list, every model in the list must support the role's effort (getModelEffortLevelsSync) — push an error naming the offending model otherwise. Weights are NORMALIZED, so do NOT require a sum of 100. seededRoles() (:301) already passes the model field verbatim, so the GET round-trip needs no change — add a test confirming a list survives GET.
- AC: PUT /api/settings validation ACCEPTS roles.work.model as a non-empty list of {model:<valid ref>, weight:<positive int>} (including workhorse refs), with no sum-to-100 requirement
- AC: Validation REJECTS a list with an invalid model ref, a non-positive or non-integer weight, an empty list, a zero total weight, or a 'parent' entry — each with a field-path error
- AC: When roles.review.effort is set and one model in roles.review.model's list does not support that effort, validation rejects with an error naming the offending model
- AC: A scalar model still validates as before, and a saved list survives the GET /api/settings round-trip (seededRoles emits it verbatim)

title: pan-1832: Validate the list form at config-load (fail fast on bad yaml)
description:
Extend config-yaml.ts load-time validation (validateRoleModelRefs at :1672 and validateRoleFields at :1647) so a list-form model is checked: non-empty; each entry has a string model and an integer weight > 0; each model ref derefs via derefWorkhorse (workhorse refs allowed); reject 'parent' as a list entry; total weight > 0. Apply to both roles[role].model and roles[role].sub[subRole].model. Throw a precise field-path error (e.g. 'config.yaml: roles.work.model[1].weight must be a positive integer') consistent with existing messages. Scalar validation path unchanged.
- AC: config-load accepts roles.work.model = [{model:'kimi-k2.7-code',weight:70},{model:'gpt-5.5',weight:30}] and the loaded config preserves the list
- AC: Loading a config whose list contains a weight of 0, a negative weight, a non-integer weight, or an empty list throws a field-path error at load time
- AC: Loading a config whose list contains an unknown/invalid model ref or a 'parent' entry throws at load time
- AC: A scalar roles.work.model still resolves exactly as before (preserves prior behavior; existing config-yaml-roles tests pass)

title: pan-1832: Add deterministic hash + weighted sampler + resolveModelForSpawn
description:
In src/lib/config-yaml.ts (or a new src/lib/role-model-distribution.ts imported by config-yaml) add: hashStringToUint32(seed: string): number — a pure FNV-1a 32-bit string hash (NO Math.random, NO Date). sampleModelRef(dist: RoleModelDistribution, seed: string): ModelRef — totalWeight = Σ positive weights; idx = hashStringToUint32(seed) % totalWeight; walk cumulative weights, return the matched entry.model (return last entry as a defensive fallback). resolveModelForSpawn(role: Role, subRole: string | undefined, seed: string, config): ModelId — mirror resolveModel's precedence: chosenConfig = (subModel !== 'parent' ? subModel : undefined) ?? roleModel ?? DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS[role]; if isModelDistribution(chosenConfig) → derefWorkhorse(sampleModelRef(chosenConfig, seed)) else derefWorkhorse(chosenConfig). When subModel==='parent' the chosen config naturally falls through to the parent role.model (which may itself be a list and gets sampled with the same seed). For a scalar config resolveModelForSpawn is a no-op equal to resolveModel.
- AC: hashStringToUint32 is pure and deterministic: the same string returns the same uint32 across calls, and the implementation contains no Math.random/Date usage (asserted by a source-level test or grep in the unit test)
- AC: sampleModelRef returns the same model for the same (dist, seed) every call; across 1000 distinct seeds with weights [70,30] the realized split is within ±5 percentage points of 70/30
- AC: sampleModelRef normalizes weights and produces identical picks for [{a,70},{b,30}] and [{a,7},{b,3}] over the same seed set
- AC: resolveModelForSpawn('work', undefined, seed, config) equals resolveModel for a scalar work config (no-op), and for a list config returns a deref'd ModelId drawn from the list; workhorse refs inside list entries deref correctly
- AC: When a sub-role model is the scalar 'parent' and the parent role.model is a list, resolveModelForSpawn samples the parent's list with the given seed (inheritance + sampling)

title: pan-1832: Document the weighted role-model distribution + config example
description:
Document the feature where role/model configuration is described (the role-models / Settings → Roles configuration doc under docs/ and/or the Mintlify configuration pages). Cover: the list form {model, weight}; normalized weights; deterministic hash-of-agent-id sampling (same agent ⇒ same model, fleet realizes the split); per-model harness mixing via resolveHarness; the flywheel-singleton caveat; and the realized-split view. Include a config.yaml example (e.g. roles.work.model = 70% kimi-k2.7-code / 30% gpt-5.5). Confirm no `pan` CLI verb/flag changed, so skills lint is unaffected — if any referenced help text changed, update the matching skills/pan-*/SKILL.md in the same bead.
- AC: The configuration doc contains the {model, weight} list form, the normalized-weights rule, deterministic agent-id sampling, and a config.yaml example (70% kimi / 30% gpt-5.5)
- AC: The configuration doc covers the flywheel-singleton caveat and the realized-split view
- AC: npm run lint (incl. scripts/lint-skills.sh) passes — no skills drift introduced

title: pan-1832: Show realized model split per role from live agents
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx add a read-only 'Realized split' display per role. Add a react-query hook fetching GET /api/agents (which already returns per-agent role + model + status); filter to live/running agents, group by role then count by model. Render under each role's picker: the live counts per model (e.g. 'live: kimi-k2.7-code ×18, gpt-5.5 ×7') next to the configured target derived from that role's distribution (e.g. 'target 70 / 30'). Reuse displayModelRef and existing styling; poll on the same staleTime/refetch pattern the panel already uses (no new endpoint, no blocking server work). Show nothing (or 'no live agents') when a role has zero running agents.
- AC: For each role the panel renders live agent counts grouped by state.model, aggregated client-side from GET /api/agents
- AC: The panel renders the realized counts alongside the configured target distribution for that role (counts vs target %)
- AC: A role with zero running agents renders an empty/'no live agents' state rather than an error
- AC: The realized-split view produces its data from the existing /api/agents response and adds no new server endpoint

title: pan-1832: Add RoleModelConfig list type and keep resolveModel deterministic
description:
In src/lib/config-yaml.ts add the distribution types and helpers, widen the role model field, and keep resolveModel() deterministic so all existing static callers are unaffected. Add: interface RoleModelWeight { model: ModelRef; weight: number }; type RoleModelDistribution = RoleModelWeight[]; type RoleModelConfig = ModelRef | RoleModelDistribution. Change RoleConfig.model and RoleSubConfig.model from ModelRef to RoleModelConfig. Add isModelDistribution(m): m is RoleModelDistribution (Array.isArray) and primaryModelRef(m): ModelRef (returns m when string, else m[0].model). Update resolveModel() (config-yaml.ts:1576) so that after picking the chosen ref-config (sub/role/default) it passes primaryModelRef(chosenConfig) into derefWorkhorse — scalar configs resolve byte-for-byte as before. Fix any type errors at scalar-assuming sites (DEFAULT_ROLES/DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS stay scalar). NO sampling here.
- AC: RoleConfig.model and RoleSubConfig.model each accepts either a scalar ref or a {model, weight} list; the RoleModelWeight/RoleModelDistribution/RoleModelConfig types exist and the project typecheck succeeds
- AC: resolveModel('work', undefined, config) returns the identical ModelId for a scalar config as before this change (back-compat preserved by an existing-behavior test)
- AC: Given a role configured with [{model:'kimi-k2.7-code',weight:70},{model:'gpt-5.5',weight:30}], resolveModel returns the first entry deref'd (kimi-k2.7-code) deterministically — proving static callers stay deterministic
- AC: primaryModelRef returns the scalar for a scalar input and list[0].model for a list input; isModelDistribution returns true only for an array

title: pan-1826: POST /api/conversations: resolve initial harness via resolveHarness({model}) when no explicit harness
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, add the exported async function resolveInitialConversationHarness(requested, model) specified verbatim in .pan/drafts/PAN-1826.md WI-C step 1 (next to resolveAllowedHarness at :411): no model → 'claude-code'; explicit valid requested harness → canUseHarnessSync-gated exactly like today; otherwise resolveHarness({ model }) with try/catch collapsing HarnessResolutionError to 'claude-code'. Import resolveHarness from ../../../lib/harness-resolve.js. Switch ONLY the POST /api/conversations creation call site at :2464 from resolveAllowedHarness to the new function. The call sites at :3588 (respawn) and :4224-4227 (fork/handoff launch/summary/author) MUST remain on resolveAllowedHarness byte-for-byte (PAN-1828 owns inheritance semantics; plain forks reject pi/codex at :4231). All added code is async-only (server-reachable file, PAN-70 rule). New test file src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/conversations-initial-harness.test.ts unit-testing the exported function per WI-C step 3, mocking ../../../lib/harness-resolve.js.
- AC: Given a new-conversation request with model 'gpt-5.5' and no harness field, resolveInitialConversationHarness consults resolveHarness({ model: 'gpt-5.5' }) (mock asserted) and returns its answer ('codex' in the test)
- AC: Given no model, the function returns 'claude-code' without consulting resolveHarness; given an explicit requested harness plus a model, it preserves today's behavior (canUseHarnessSync-gated, falls back to 'claude-code' when denied); given resolveHarness throwing HarnessResolutionError, it falls back to 'claude-code'
- AC: Only the :2464 creation call site routes through the new function — the respawn (:3588) and fork/handoff (:4224-4227) call sites still call resolveAllowedHarness and the route preserves today's fork/handoff/respawn behavior (existing conversations route tests stay green)
- AC: The diff contains no execSync or other sync child_process usage (server-reachable code; NFR-1)
close_reason: Replaced the sync CLI resolver in POST /api/conversations with an async auth/config resolver built on loadConfigNoMigration(), getClaudeAuthStatus(), and getOpenAIAuthStatus; verified helper test, typecheck, and eslint.

title: pan-1826: pan strike --dry-run: print the resolver's harness instead of hardcoded claude-code fallback
description:
In src/cli/commands/strike.ts dry-run block (lines 119-129), compute the real spawn outcome before printing: dryRunModel = resolveRoleModel('strike', options.model || undefined, loadYamlConfig().config) (import resolveModel as resolveRoleModel and loadConfigSync as loadYamlConfig from ../../lib/config-yaml.js, mirroring start.ts:13), then dryRunHarness = await resolveHarness({ explicit: options.harness, role: 'strike', model: dryRunModel }) (import from ../../lib/harness-resolve.js). Replace the `Harness: ${options.harness ?? 'claude-code'}` line with dryRunHarness and replace the conditional `if (options.model)` model line with an unconditional `Model: ${dryRunModel}`. Do not touch the non-dry-run spawn path (strike.ts:139 already passes options.harness raw). Test in src/cli/commands/__tests__/strike.test.ts: mock ../../lib/harness-resolve.js with resolveHarness: vi.fn(async () => 'codex'); run dry-run without --harness; assert output contains 'Harness:    codex' and resolveHarness was called with explicit: undefined.
- AC: Given pan strike --dry-run with no --harness and a strike model whose resolver answer is codex (mocked), the dry-run output renders 'Harness:    codex' and resolveHarness receives explicit: undefined, role: 'strike'
- AC: Dry-run output prints the resolved strike model unconditionally (resolveRoleModel('strike', ...) result), even when --model was not passed
- AC: The non-dry-run strike spawn path is unchanged: spawnAgent still receives harness: options.harness (raw, possibly undefined) — diff for this bead touches only the dry-run block, imports, and tests
close_reason: Resolved strike dry-run model and harness through the same resolver path used for spawns; added focused dry-run coverage.

title: pan-1826: pan start: forward --harness only when actually passed; let resolveHarness() own flagless spawns
description:
In src/cli/commands/start.ts, replace lines 704-722 (the `requestedHarness = options.harness ?? 'claude-code'` coalesce + always-on validation) with the async helper resolveExplicitHarnessFlag(harness, model) specified verbatim in .pan/drafts/PAN-1826.md WI-A: returns undefined when the flag is absent; on a present flag, exits 1 for invalid values (same 'Invalid --harness value:' stderr text) and, when --model is also given, exits 1 with the canUseHarnessSync reason for disallowed combos. Pass its result (RuntimeName | undefined) as `harness:` in the spawnAgent call at start.ts:1120-1129. Update the stale doc comment at start.ts:89-90. Add the helper to the __testInternals export at start.ts:1176. Extend src/cli/commands/__tests__/start-harness.test.ts with the four cases listed in WI-A step 4 (flagless → undefined; flagless+gpt-5.5 → undefined without pre-gating; invalid value exits 1; existing two issueCommand tests stay green unchanged). Do NOT duplicate the codex-resolution tests — they exist at tests/unit/lib/harness-resolve.test.ts:90 and src/lib/__tests__/agents-spawn-supervisor.test.ts:301-325.
- AC: Given no --harness flag, __testInternals.resolveExplicitHarnessFlag(undefined, undefined) and (undefined, 'gpt-5.5') both resolve to undefined and the process does not exit (new tests pass)
- AC: The spawnAgent call in issueCommand passes the helper result directly: a flagless `pan start` forwards harness: undefined, so resolveHarness({explicit: undefined, role: 'work', model}) picks the provider default (codex for openai models, per existing agents-spawn-supervisor.test.ts:301-325 which stays green)
- AC: pan start --harness cursor still exits 1 with 'Invalid --harness value:' on stderr, and --harness pi --model claude-sonnet-4-6 under subscription auth still exits 1 with the canUseHarnessSync reason (existing start-harness.test.ts cases pass unmodified)
- AC: Given no --harness flag, the upfront canUseHarnessSync pre-gate is skipped entirely (no early exit for any flagless model value); policy enforcement falls through to resolveHarness inside spawnAgent
close_reason: Forwarded undefined for flagless pan start harness resolution, preserved explicit flag validation, and added focused regression coverage.

title: pan-1812: pan pause refuses flywheel-invoked pauses of non-flywheel-started work agents behind a default-off toggle
description:
Add FLYWHEEL_GOVERNOR_REAP_OPERATOR_AGENTS_KEY = 'flywheel.governor_reap_operator_agents' to src/lib/database/app-settings.ts with isFlywheelGovernorReapOperatorAgents()/setFlywheelGovernorReapOperatorAgents() following the isFlywheelAutoPickupBacklog pattern (lines 104-118; default false). In src/cli/commands/pause.ts pauseCommand (lines 10-45), after resolving target state and before any mutation: if process.env.PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID === FLYWHEEL_ORCHESTRATOR_AGENT_ID AND state.role === 'work' AND state.startedBy !== 'flywheel' AND the toggle is false, print a policy error naming the config key and exit(1). Legacy agents without startedBy are protected (treated as operator-started). Update skills/pan-pause/SKILL.md if it documents behavior the lint cross-checks. Full spec: WI-7 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1812.md.
- AC: app-settings stores flywheel.governor_reap_operator_agents via a set helper, and the is helper returns false when the setting is absent (defaults to off)
- AC: pauseCommand under PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID=flywheel-orchestrator targeting a work agent whose startedBy is not 'flywheel' exits non-zero with a message naming the config key, and the target state remains unpaused
- AC: The same flywheel invocation succeeds against a startedBy='flywheel' agent, and succeeds against operator-started agents when the toggle is set true
- AC: Given no PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID in the environment, pauseCommand marks any resolvable agent paused exactly as before

title: pan-1812: Initiator threads through plan kickoff, planning state, finalize, and auto-spawn
description:
Carry initiator explicitly across the two HTTP hops where env detection breaks: (1) src/cli/commands/plan.ts detects initiator from env (same resolution as agent-started-by) and includes it in the planning kickoff request body; (2) routes/issues.ts planning kickoff (lines ~845-1070) accepts initiator and passes it into spawnPlanningSession input (spawn-planning-session.ts:129-161); (3) buildPlanningAgentState persists startedBy beside autoSpawnOnFinalize (spawn-planning-session.ts:681-689); (4) plan-finalize.ts reads it back like readAutoSpawnOnFinalize (lines 82-92) and sends it in the promotePlanning POST body (line 299+); (5) completePlanningAutoSpawn (routes/issues.ts:253-302) forwards initiator in the work-agent spawn request and the agents start route (routes/agents.ts ~2514+, which shells pan start) appends --initiator <value>. Full spec: WI-6 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1812.md.
- AC: pan plan invoked with PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID=flywheel-orchestrator produces a planning agent state.json containing startedBy='flywheel'
- AC: plan-finalize reads the planning startedBy and sends it in the complete-planning request body (unit test on the read-back helper and request payload)
- AC: completePlanningAutoSpawn forwards initiator and the agents start route appends --initiator when shelling pan start (test asserts the spawn request/command at each seam)
- AC: A work agent auto-spawned from flywheel-initiated planning persists startedBy='flywheel'; operator-initiated planning produces 'operator'

title: pan-1812: Fresh .beads/issues.jsonl mtime clears the stuck ladder before any stage fires
description:
In evaluateWorkAgent (src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts, after the review-status/idle guards and before stage evaluation), stat join(agent.workspace, '.beads', 'issues.jsonl') in a try/catch. If mtimeMs is newer than stuckState.firstStuckAt (or, when no stuck state exists, within the past config.stage1_minutes relative to the now parameter), call clearStuckRemediationState(agentId) and return. Missing/unreadable beads file falls through to existing behavior. This is the independent activity evidence that would have prevented the 18:34Z false positive (idleMin=90 while the agent closed beads at 18:31 and 18:38). Use the existing now-parameter test pattern; no real timers. Full spec: WI-4 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1812.md.
- AC: Given a beads file mtime newer than firstStuckAt, evaluateWorkAgent clears stuck-remediation state and returns without any stage transition
- AC: Given a missing or stale beads file, the ladder behaves exactly as before (existing stage-transition tests keep passing)

title: pan-1812: Stuck-remediation stage 3 pauses work agents with a reason instead of marking troubled
description:
In src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts evaluateWorkAgent stage-3 branch (lines 117-122), replace markAgentTroubled(agentId) with setAgentPausedSync(agentId, `stuck-remediation stage 3: idle ${idleMinutes} min with no remaining beads for ${issueId}`, false) — third arg false so the process is not flagged stopped (stage 3 today does not kill). Rename the logged action from 'marked-troubled' to 'paused-no-remaining-beads'. Do NOT touch evaluateFlywheelOrchestrator (lines 181-188) — the orchestrator's own paused-and-troubled stage 3 is out of scope. The existing guard at line 84 (paused/troubled => skip) keeps the ladder from re-firing. Full spec: WI-3 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1812.md.
- AC: Stage 3 for a work agent marks the agent paused and persists a pausedReason containing 'stuck-remediation' and the idle minutes, while troubled stays unset
- AC: The stage-3 deacon log line emits action=paused-no-remaining-beads instead of marked-troubled
- AC: evaluateFlywheelOrchestrator stage 3 still pauses the flywheel and marks it troubled (existing tests keep passing unchanged)

title: pan-1812: Flywheel brief prescribes verified completion checks and limits slot reaping to flywheel-launched agents
description:
In docs/flywheel-brief.md add a 'Governor: slots and reaping' subsection and extend the Do-not list (lines 95-113): maxAgents governs flywheel-initiated launches, not authority to reap operator-started agents; pan pause is permitted only against agents the flywheel itself launched (CLI-enforced — a refusal is policy, not a bug to work around); before asserting any agent's work is complete, run the verified workspace-scoped check `cd workspaces/feature-<id> && bd list -l <issue-id-lowercase>` where in_progress beads are active work and a bd error means unknown, never complete; bare `bd list` without -l counts other issues' beads inherited from main and must not be used; pause reasons must state the real rationale and never claim work-complete without the verified check. Keep the imperative voice of the existing brief. Full spec: WI-8 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1812.md.
- AC: docs/flywheel-brief.md contains a governor subsection that prescribes the workspace-scoped, label-filtered bd check and states that in_progress beads are active work and bd errors mean unknown
- AC: The Do-not list contains the rule that pan pause is permitted only against flywheel-launched agents and that slot caps govern flywheel-initiated launches only
- AC: The brief contains the requirement that pause reasons give the real rationale and never claim work-complete without the verified check

title: pan-1812: AgentState.startedBy provenance recorded at spawn with env detection in pan start
description:
Add startedBy?: 'operator' | 'flywheel' to AgentState in src/lib/agents.ts (~line 934) AND to the cleanAgentState field whitelist (~line 945) — omitting the whitelist entry silently drops the field on save. Add startedBy to SpawnOptions and persist in spawnAgent (default 'operator'). In src/cli/commands/start.ts add internal option --initiator <operator|flywheel> registered in src/cli/index.ts; resolution order: explicit flag, then env (process.env.PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID === FLYWHEEL_ORCHESTRATOR_AGENT_ID from src/lib/cloister/flywheel.ts:18 => 'flywheel'), else 'operator'; pass into the spawnAgent call at start.ts:1120. If importing the constant creates an import cycle, move it to a leaf module rather than inlining the literal. Update skills/pan-start/SKILL.md in the same commit if it enumerates flags (scripts/lint-skills.sh cross-checks --help). Full spec: WI-5 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1812.md.
- AC: A spawned agent's state.json contains startedBy, and the value survives a save/load round-trip through cleanAgentState
- AC: pan start running with PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID=flywheel-orchestrator records startedBy='flywheel'; with no PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID it defaults to 'operator'

title: pan-1812: Tri-state getWorkspaceBeadWork helper replaces hasReadyBeads in the stuck ladder
description:
Create leaf module src/lib/cloister/bead-work.ts exporting getWorkspaceBeadWork(workspace, issueLabel): Promise<'has-work'|'no-work'|'unknown'>. It runs `bd list --json -l <issueLabel>` via execFileAsync with cwd=workspace and timeout 10000ms; >=1 bead with status open or in_progress => 'has-work'; 0 => 'no-work'; any exec error, timeout, or JSON parse failure => 'unknown' with a logDeaconEventSync line. In src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts, replace the hasReadyBeads call at line 96: 'has-work' => return (deacon bead-nudge patrol owns that case); 'unknown' => log and return without escalation; only 'no-work' proceeds down the ladder. Delete hasReadyBeads (lines 40-49). Tests mock child_process execFile. Full spec: WI-2 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1812.md.
- AC: getWorkspaceBeadWork returns 'has-work' when bd list --json -l <label> run with cwd=workspace yields at least one open or in_progress bead
- AC: getWorkspaceBeadWork returns 'unknown' (never 'no-work') when bd exits non-zero, times out, or emits unparseable JSON, and logs the failure
- AC: evaluateWorkAgent returns without any stage transition when the helper yields 'has-work' or 'unknown' (unit test exercises both)
- AC: The stuck ladder proceeds to stage evaluation only when the helper yields 'no-work', and hasReadyBeads no longer exists in stuck-remediation.ts

title: pan-1812: checkTaskCompletion queries the workspace beads DB and treats bd errors as unknown
description:
In src/lib/cloister/triggers.ts checkTaskCompletionPromise (lines 289-302), pass `cwd: workspace` to both execAsync('bd list ...') calls when workspace is provided. Change the remaining-work query so in_progress beads count as remaining work (the current `--status open` query at line 300 misses them). Keep the catch-to-not-triggered behavior but reword the fallback reason to say beads state was unavailable/unknown, never implying completion. Callers already pass workspace (cloister/service.ts:1588, routes/agents.ts:2324). Full spec: WI-1 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1812.md.
- AC: Given a workspace argument, both bd list invocations in checkTaskCompletionPromise execute with exec options cwd equal to that workspace (unit test asserts the exec call options)
- AC: Given a bead in in_progress status for the issue label, checkTaskCompletion returns triggered:false with a reason naming remaining tasks
- AC: When the bd command fails, checkTaskCompletion returns triggered:false with a reason stating beads state is unknown/unavailable, never a completion claim

title: pan-1801: Write docs/CI-FEEDBACK-RELAY.md describing the relay contract
description:
Create docs/CI-FEEDBACK-RELAY.md (~60 lines) covering: trigger events (check_suite terminal non-success, status failure/error; explicitly NOT check_run), per-(issueId, headSha) debounce and 60s settle timer, the live-agent gate and no-auto-resume rule, feedback file shape (NNN-ci-monitor-ci-failure.md) and MUST READ nudge, the inherited-from-base rule including all-inherited suppression and fail-open on fetch errors, log-excerpt caps (3 runs / 80 lines / 8KB), and how this relates to the deacon's merge-time checkFailedMergeRetry path (deacon.ts) and the open PAN-1658 testStatus reconciler gap.
- AC: docs/CI-FEEDBACK-RELAY.md covers trigger events, debounce and settle semantics, the live-agent gate, log-excerpt caps, and the inherited-failure rule including suppression and fail-open
- AC: The doc contains a section distinguishing the relay's PR-open window from the deacon's merge-time checkFailedMergeRetry path and references PAN-1658 as the adjacent unsolved gap

metadata.acJustification: Documentation bead — two ACs pin the required content; no runtime behavior to test.

title: pan-1801: Invoke scheduleCiFailureRelay from check_suite and status failure branches
description:
In src/lib/webhook-handlers.ts: (1) handleCheckSuitePromise — in the terminal non-success branch (the addFailingSource call at :275), after setReviewStatus completes, call scheduleCiFailureRelay({issueId, repo, prNumber: pr.number, headSha: pr.head.sha ?? status.prHeadSha}); skip the call when both SHAs are undefined. (2) handleStatusPromise — in the state failure/error branch (:534-536), derive prNumber = status.prNumber ?? prNumberFromUrl(status.prUrl ?? '') and headSha = payload.sha ?? status.prHeadSha; call scheduleCiFailureRelay only when both are present. (3) handleCheckRunPromise — unchanged, no relay call. Import scheduleCiFailureRelay statically from ./cloister/ci-failure-relay.js (heavy deps inside the relay are dynamic-imported, so no cycle; if Node strict ESM still reports one, apply the stated fallback of hosting FAILING_CHECK_CONCLUSIONS in the relay module). Extend tests/unit/lib/webhook-handlers.test.ts with a vi.mock of the relay module.
- AC: Given a check_suite webhook with conclusion 'failure' for a tracked PR, handleCheckSuite sends scheduleCiFailureRelay the issue id, repo, PR number, and head SHA
- AC: Given a check_suite webhook with conclusion 'success', handleCheckSuite removes the failing source and never invokes scheduleCiFailureRelay
- AC: Given a status webhook with state 'failure' and a stored prNumber/prHeadSha, handleStatus invokes scheduleCiFailureRelay; when prNumber or headSha cannot be derived it skips the call
- AC: Given a failing check_run webhook, handleCheckRun updates blockers but never invokes scheduleCiFailureRelay

title: pan-1801: Partition PR failures against the base branch's failing checks; suppress all-inherited relays
description:
In ci-failure-relay.ts: fetch the base branch failing set via gh api repos/<repo>/commits/<baseRefName>/check-runs?per_page=100 (execFile, 15s timeout; baseRefName comes from the gh pr view call already made in relay-core); build the set of check-run names whose conclusion is in the failing set; partition the PR's failing checks by name membership. Render inherited failures in a separate section titled 'Inherited from <baseRefName> — these are failing on <baseRefName> itself; do not chase them'. If every PR failure is inherited, log '[ci-relay] all failures inherited from <base> — suppressed' and return before writing feedback or messaging the agent. If the base fetch throws, proceed with all failures listed as the PR's own and append the note 'could not compare against <baseRefName> — some failures may be inherited'. Extend the relay unit tests for all three behaviors.
- AC: Given a PR failure whose check name also fails on the base branch HEAD, the feedback body renders it under an 'Inherited from <base>' section instead of the own-failures list
- AC: Given every PR failure is inherited, the relay logs a suppression line and writes no feedback file and sends no message
- AC: Given the base-branch check-runs fetch throws, the relay still writes feedback listing all failures as the PR's own and the body contains a could-not-compare note

title: pan-1801: Fetch truncated failing-job log excerpts into the feedback body
description:
In ci-failure-relay.ts, after the failing set is known: gh run list --commit <headSha> --repo <repo> --json databaseId,name,workflowName,conclusion (execFile, 15s timeout); select runs whose conclusion is in the failing set; for at most 3, gh run view <databaseId> --repo <repo> --log-failed; keep only the last 80 lines per run and enforce an 8KB total budget across runs, appending a '[truncated]' annotation line whenever a cap trims output; append the excerpts to the feedback markdown under per-run headings (workflowName / run id). When gh run list returns no runs (external CI via commit statuses) or any gh call fails, fall back to the names-only body from relay-core plus a one-line note that logs were unavailable. Extend tests in tests/unit/lib/cloister/ci-failure-relay.test.ts with mocked execFile outputs.
- AC: Given 4 failed Actions runs on the head SHA, the feedback body contains log excerpts for exactly 3 runs, each fetched via gh run view --log-failed
- AC: Given a run log exceeding 80 lines or excerpts exceeding 8KB total, the body preserves only the tail within the caps and contains a truncation annotation
- AC: Given gh run list returns an empty list or the gh call throws, the relay falls back to a body listing each failing check name and conclusion plus a logs-unavailable note

title: pan-1801: Create ci-failure-relay module: debounce, settle timer, live-agent gate, feedback write + nudge
description:
New file src/lib/cloister/ci-failure-relay.ts. Export scheduleCiFailureRelay(opts: {issueId: string; repo: string; prNumber: number; headSha: string}): void — synchronous: if debounce Map already has key `${issueId}:${headSha}` return; otherwise mark the key (Map bounded to 1000 entries, evict oldest insertion), then setTimeout(60_000).unref() invoking relayCiFailureNow(opts).catch(console.warn). relayCiFailureNow: (1) live-agent gate — dynamic-import ../tmux.js, sessionExistsSync(`agent-${issueId.toLowerCase()}`); absent => console.log and return; (2) gh pr view <prNumber> --repo <repo> --json statusCheckRollup,baseRefName,headRefOid via promisified execFile (timeout 15000); (3) abort if headRefOid !== opts.headSha; (4) collect failing checks from statusCheckRollup using FAILING_CHECK_CONCLUSIONS exported from webhook-handlers.ts:174 (fallback if an ESM cycle appears: define the const here and re-export from webhook-handlers); empty failing set => return; (5) build markdown body listing each failing check name + conclusion (log excerpts and inherited partition arrive in later beads); (6) dynamic-import ./feedback-writer.js, writeFeedbackFile({issueId, specialist: 'ci-monitor', outcome: 'ci-failure', summary, markdownBody}); (7) dynamic-import ../agents.js, deliverAgentMessage(`agent-${issueId.toLowerCase()}`, message containing 'MUST READ: <absolute feedback path>' plus 'fix, commit, push — Panopticon detects new commits and re-runs the pipeline', 'ci-failure-relay'). Also export _resetCiFailureRelayForTests() clearing the Map and pending timers (mirror clearAllReconciliationTimeouts, webhook-handlers.ts:179). Tests at tests/unit/lib/cloister/ci-failure-relay.test.ts with vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(60000) (repo rule: never real timers) and vi.mock of child_process, ../tmux.js, ./feedback-writer.js, ../agents.js.
- AC: Given two scheduleCiFailureRelay calls with the same issueId and headSha, when 60s elapse under fake timers, then writeFeedbackFile receives exactly one call; a different headSha produces a second relay
- AC: Given no agent-<issue> tmux session, when the settle timer fires, the relay returns after a log line — writes no feedback file and sends no agent message
- AC: Given gh pr view returns a headRefOid different from the scheduled headSha, the relay exits without writing feedback or messaging the agent
- AC: Given a live session and matching head with failing checks, the relay creates .pan/feedback/NNN-ci-monitor-ci-failure.md via writeFeedbackFile and sends a deliverAgentMessage whose text contains the absolute feedback file path
- AC: scheduleCiFailureRelay returns synchronously with an unref'd timer, and a rejection inside relayCiFailureNow logs a warning instead of propagating to the caller

title: pan-1801: Add 'ci-monitor' to the two specialist unions
description:
Append | 'ci-monitor' to WriteFeedbackOptions.specialist at src/lib/cloister/feedback-writer.ts:19 and to ContinueFeedbackEntry.specialist at src/lib/vbrief/continue-state.ts:93 (search for the union string 'verification-gate' | 'review-agent' if lines drifted). No other changes — filename generation (NNN-<specialist>-<outcome>.md) and continue-file append already handle arbitrary union members.
- AC: Given a writeFeedbackFile call with specialist 'ci-monitor', npm run typecheck accepts it and exits 0
- AC: ContinueFeedbackEntry.specialist in src/lib/vbrief/continue-state.ts accepts 'ci-monitor' so appendFeedbackEntryForIssue persists relay entries without type errors

metadata.acJustification: Two one-line union extensions verified by typecheck; two ACs cover both edit sites.

title: pan-1797: Unit tests for resume-path origin writes and non-explicit resolver call
description:
Add resumeAgent-path tests to src/lib/__tests__/agents-spawn-supervisor.test.ts, which already mocks resolveHarness (resolveHarnessMock) and emitAgentEvent (emitAgentEventMock) — follow the origin-write assertion patterns at lines 331-420. Set up a stopped resumable agent (agent dir with state.json status 'stopped', session.id file, initial-prompt.md, existing workspace dir — mirror the writeResumableWorkAgent shape from tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts:344-366, plus a runtime snapshot mock for origin state). Tests: (1) fresh respawn after origin drift emits a model_set event with expect.objectContaining({ sessionModel: <new model>, sessionHarness: <new harness> }); (2) a matching-origin --resume emits no model_set call having sessionModel/sessionHarness properties (use the mock.calls.find pattern at lines 418-420); (3) a legacy resume (snapshot without origin fields) calls resolveHarness without an explicit key — assert the call argument object has no 'explicit' own-property (or explicit: undefined, matching the chosen implementation from the legacy-harness-re-resolution bead).
- AC: Given a drift-triggered fresh respawn through resumeAgent, then the test asserts emitAgentEvent receives a model_set event containing both sessionModel and sessionHarness for the new session
- AC: Given a normal --resume relaunch with matching origin, then the test asserts no model_set call contains sessionModel or sessionHarness properties
- AC: Given a legacy agent resumed with no opts, then the test asserts resolveHarness receives no explicit harness (role and model only)
close_reason: Added resumeAgent unit coverage for fresh origin writes, matching-origin no-rewrite, and legacy non-explicit resolveHarness; verified unit test file and typecheck.

title: pan-1797: Integration tests for legacy harness migration in agent-spawning.test.ts
description:
Add integration tests to tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts next to the PAN-1787 drift tests (lines 577-631). Extend writeResumableWorkAgent (line 344) with optional model/harness overrides. Use the stub-binary PATH pattern (lines 917-923: write executable '#!/bin/sh\nexit 0' stub into a temp bin dir, prepend to process.env.PATH, then call resetHarnessResolveCachesForTests()). Tests: (1) legacy origin (bare setRuntimeOrigin(agentId)) + state.model gpt-5.5 + stub codex binary → resumeAgent(agentId) yields launcher without --resume, state.json harness codex, codexMode work-tui, lifecycle log contains the migration line; (2) legacy + state.model kimi-k2.5 + stub pi binary + process.env.KIMI_API_KEY set (delete in finally, pattern at line 326) → fresh session with harness pi; (3) the existing legacy test at line 621 (anthropic model → equal) passes unmodified; (4) legacy + kimi model + explicit opts.harness 'claude-code' → no migration, launcher preserves --resume. Note: cliproxy is already mocked running for gpt-5.5; PANOPTICON_PROMPT_READY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=1 is already set in beforeEach — do not add real-time waits (fake-timers rule).
- AC: Given a legacy gpt-5.5 agent with a stubbed codex binary, when resumed with no opts, then the test asserts the launcher contains no --resume, state.json harness equals codex, and the lifecycle log contains the migration line
- AC: Given a legacy kimi-k2.5 agent with a stubbed pi binary, when resumed with no opts, then the test asserts a fresh session launches with harness pi persisted in state.json
- AC: Given the existing PAN-1787 legacy backward-compat test (line 621), when the full suite runs, then it passes unmodified, and a new explicit-harness test asserts --resume is preserved when opts.harness is supplied for a legacy agent
- AC: Given any in-test PATH mutation, then the test calls resetHarnessResolveCachesForTests() before resolution so binary availability caching survives across tests deterministically
close_reason: Added integration coverage for legacy gpt→codex migration, kimi→pi migration, and explicit harness preserving --resume; verified integration test file and typecheck.

title: pan-1797: Persist session-origin metadata on resumeAgent fresh respawns
description:
In resumeAgent() (src/lib/agents.ts), extend the existing post-launch saveAgentRuntimeState call at lines 4807-4810 to additionally include sessionModel: model and sessionHarness: effectiveHarness when !shouldResumeSavedSession (i.e. any fresh respawn: compact recovery, PAN-1787 drift, or PAN-1797 migration). Both fields must be written in the same patch — the model_set reducer keeps prior values via '?? prev' (packages/contracts/src/event-reducers.ts:1088-1096), so a single-field write would half-update origin. Normal --resume relaunches must NOT write origin fields. Exact snippet in .pan/drafts/PAN-1797.md § Proposal.
- AC: Given resumeAgent launches a fresh session (drift or migration), then it emits a model_set event carrying both sessionModel equal to the resumed model and sessionHarness equal to the effective harness
- AC: Given resumeAgent re-attaches with --resume (no drift, no migration), then it emits no model_set event containing sessionModel or sessionHarness fields (prior origin preserved)
close_reason: Persisted sessionModel and sessionHarness together on resumeAgent fresh respawns only; verified typecheck and agent-spawning integration tests.

title: pan-1797: Re-resolve harness in resumeAgent for legacy sessions lacking origin metadata
description:
In resumeAgent() (src/lib/agents.ts), replace the harness resolution at lines 4655-4660 per the exact before/after snippet in .pan/drafts/PAN-1797.md § Proposal: compute storedHarness = agentState.harness ?? 'claude-code', isLegacySessionOrigin = !runtimeState?.sessionModel || !runtimeState?.sessionHarness, migrateLegacyHarness = opts?.harness === undefined && isLegacySessionOrigin; call resolveHarness WITHOUT the explicit key when migrating (otherwise keep explicit: opts?.harness ?? agentState.harness); compute legacyMigrationReason = 'legacy harness migration <old>→<new>' when migrating and the result differs from storedHarness. After the sessionResumeDriftReasons call at line 4668, push legacyMigrationReason onto resumeDriftReasons and emit exactly one migration log line via console.log and logAgentLifecycleSync naming old harness, new harness, and model. All downstream fresh-session plumbing (shouldResumeSavedSession, freshSessionId, session.id unlink, defaultResumeMessage, kickoff-style delivery, fresh-respawn logging at 4800-4802) is reused unchanged. The existing agentState.harness = effectiveHarness (4660) and codexMode handling (4661-4665) already persist the adopted harness.
- AC: Given a stopped agent with no session-origin metadata, state harness claude-code, and model gpt-5.5 with a codex binary present, when resumeAgent runs with no opts, then the launcher contains no --resume, state.json harness updates to codex, and codexMode updates to work-tui
- AC: Given the same legacy agent, when the migration fires, then the agent lifecycle log records exactly one line containing 'legacy harness migration claude-code→codex' and the model id
- AC: Given a legacy agent whose model re-resolves to its stored harness (anthropic model + claude-code), when resumeAgent runs with no opts, then it preserves --resume with the saved session id (existing test at tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts:621 passes unmodified)
- AC: Given a legacy agent resumed with caller-explicit opts.harness, when resumeAgent runs, then resolveHarness receives that harness as explicit and the migration does not fire (--resume preserved absent drift)
- AC: Given an agent WITH session-origin metadata, when resumeAgent runs, then resolution still passes explicit: opts?.harness ?? agentState.harness and PAN-1787 drift behavior is preserved (existing drift tests at agent-spawning.test.ts:577-619 pass unmodified)
close_reason: Implemented resumeAgent legacy harness re-resolution, migration drift reason, and dedicated migration logging; verified typecheck and agent-spawning integration tests.

title: pan-1696: Docs sweep: UAT-BATCH-TRAINS, MERGE-TRAIN §7, FLYWHEEL scope disambiguation

(3) docs/FLYWHEEL.md — short section disambiguating Flywheel orchestrator scope (which projects the orchestrator inventories) from per-project merge/UAT train independence, stating cross-project batches remain out of scope: batches are per project; only the view/control surface spans projects.

- AC: docs/FLYWHEEL.md contains a section distinguishing orchestrator scope from per-project batch-train independence, stating cross-project batches remain out of scope
