title: pan-1696: New /api/merge-train/* routes (aggregate, per-project payloads)
description:
Create src/dashboard/server/routes/merge-train.ts with: GET /api/merge-train/queues -> Array<{ projectKey, projectName, enabled (per-project effective via isMergeTrainEnabledForProject), queue: MergeQueueItem[] }> across tracked projects; GET /api/merge-train/generations -> the per-project payload from getUatGenerationsPayload(); POST /api/merge-train/generations/:name/stack and /promote (same handlers/behavior as the existing uat-generation routes in routes/flywheel.ts:919-967, including uatGenerationNameFromParam reconstruction, rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest, and the firePostMergeLifecycle wiring on promote); POST /api/merge-train/assemble body { project? } -> forced reconcile (one project or all); POST /api/merge-train/merge-next body { n, project } -> escape-hatch merges ordered from that project's ready set (adapt postFlywheelMergeNextPayload/defaultGetOrderedIssueIds at routes/flywheel.ts:650-685 to candidates), rejecting unknown project keys with a 4xx error body. Compose the layer in src/dashboard/server/server.ts. Do NOT remove the legacy flywheel routes here — that is the remove-legacy-routes bead, after the frontend migrates. Add route tests in a new merge-train test file modeled on src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts.
- AC: GET /api/merge-train/queues returns one entry per tracked project with projectKey, effective enabled flag, and that project's queue — with no flywheel run active
- AC: GET /api/merge-train/generations returns per-project generation chains, and POST stack/promote/assemble reproduce the existing flywheel routes' behaviors under the same auth guards
- AC: POST /api/merge-train/merge-next { n, project } merges from the named project's ready set (not the flywheel pipeline) and rejects an unknown project key with a 4xx error body
close_reason: Implemented aggregate /api/merge-train routes in ad46e15fe; route/service tests, typecheck, and lint passed.

title: pan-1696: GET/POST /api/projects/:key/merge-train override endpoint
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts, beside the auto-merge-default routes (lines 573-585), add GET/POST /api/projects/:projectKey/merge-train with payload { value: 'enabled' | 'disabled' | null }. GET reads getProjectSync(key)?.merge_train ?? null and also returns the resolved effective state ({ value, effective: boolean }) so UIs can display what 'inherit' resolves to; POST validates the union (400 otherwise) and writes via setProjectMergeTrainSync. Mirror the auto-merge-default handlers' auth/validation shape exactly. Add a route test beside the existing projects route tests.
- AC: GET returns { value, effective } reflecting projects.yaml and the resolved global fallback; POST persists 'enabled'/'disabled' and rejects any other non-null value with 400
- AC: POSTing { value: null } deletes the override so the project falls back to the global flag, and the next GET returns the updated effective field
close_reason: Implemented GET/POST /api/projects/:projectKey/merge-train override route with tests; focused route test, typecheck, and lint pass.

title: pan-1696: Multi-project UAT train service: iterate tracked projects, no flywheel dependency
description:
In src/dashboard/server/services/uat-train.ts: remove projectRoot()=process.cwd() (lines 58-60) and all readCurrentFlywheelStatusForDashboard usage (lines 42, 63-84, 237). Extract buildIssueTitleMap from src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts:421-446 into a shared server helper (services/issue-title-map.ts) used by both call sites. runUatTrainReconcile: enumerate listProjectsSync(); for each project where isMergeTrainEnabledForProject(key) is true: build the ready set via listEligibleCandidatesByProject (titleFor from the title map) + computeMergeQueueFromCandidates(candidates, project.path); skip the project with ZERO git calls when candidates are empty AND listUatGenerationsSync({ projectRoot: project.path, statuses: ['ready','superseded','assembling'] }) is empty — a project whose ready set went empty but still has live generations MUST proceed so invalidation/cleanup runs; otherwise call reconcileUatGenerations(project.path, deps) with per-project-rooted deps (buildUatGenerationGitDeps(project.path); assemble/cleanup parametrized on that root). Return a per-project result map. getUatGenerationsPayload(): drop the no-run early return; return Array<{ projectKey, projectName, generations: UatGenerationPayload[] }> across tracked projects. Keep codenameLabel = issue prefix and add the D7 invariant comment (uat_generations.name is a global PK; prefix-distinct labels prevent cross-project collisions). Update src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/uat-train.test.ts with fake deps simulating two projects. The reconciler interval wiring in main.ts is unchanged.
- AC: Given no flywheel run is active and merge-train is effectively enabled, a tick produces a uat/* generation for a ready non-PAN issue in that issue's own project (test with fake deps simulating two projects)
- AC: When a project has an empty ready set and no live generations, the tick invokes zero git operations (spy deps record no calls)
- AC: When a project's ready set goes empty while live generations exist, the next tick marks them invalidated and tears down their stacks
- AC: A project carrying merge_train: 'disabled' is skipped while other enabled projects still reconcile in the same tick, and getUatGenerationsPayload() returns per-project chains tagged with projectKey/projectName while no flywheel run is active
close_reason: Implemented multi-project UAT train reconciliation in 0326b03b1; focused server/frontend tests, typecheck, and lint passed.

title: pan-1696: pan flywheel status merge queue sourced from the pipeline ready set
description:
In src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts (import at line 35, call at ~818): replace computeMergeQueue(status.activePipeline, cwd) with the cwd project's pipeline ready set — findProjectByPathSync(cwd) for the project key, listEligibleCandidatesByProject() filtered to that key, then computeMergeQueueFromCandidates(candidates, cwd). Render the same queue columns as today; the section now also renders when no run is live. No CLI flags change, so no wrapper-skill update is required (scripts/lint-skills.sh unaffected).
- AC: pan flywheel status prints the merge-queue section from the cwd project's ready set when eligible issues exist and no flywheel run is active
- AC: The command exits 0 and renders an empty queue (not an error) when no issues are eligible
close_reason: Implemented pipeline-ready merge queue for pan flywheel status in 1ef77f65a; CLI test, typecheck, and lint passed.

title: pan-1696: Per-project merge_train override in projects.yaml + resolution helper + sibling-reconciler gate
description:
Add merge_train?: 'enabled' | 'disabled' to ProjectConfig (src/lib/projects.ts:88, near auto_merge_default) and setProjectMergeTrainSync(key, value|null) mirroring setProjectAutoMergeDefaultSync (src/lib/projects.ts:241). Add isMergeTrainEnabledForProject(projectKey): per-project override when present, else isMergeTrainEnabled() — place beside getProjectAutoMergeDefault in src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-policy.ts (same resolution-tier pattern). Gate the post-merge sibling reconciler: runMergeTrainReconcile (src/lib/cloister/merge-train.ts:26-35) resolves the merged issue's project (resolveProjectFromIssueSync, as merge-train-deps.ts:22 already does) and uses the per-project helper, keeping the injectable opts.enabled test override. Unit tests in tests/unit/lib/cloister/auto-merge-policy.test.ts and merge-train.test.ts.
- AC: Resolver precedence (unit-tested): project 'disabled' wins over global ON; project 'enabled' wins over global OFF; an absent override falls back to the global flag
- AC: setProjectMergeTrainSync persists 'enabled'/'disabled' to projects.yaml and removes the field when passed null
- AC: runMergeTrainReconcile returns [] without loading git deps when the merged issue's project carries merge_train: 'disabled', even with the global flag on
close_reason: Added per-project merge_train override config, resolver precedence, and project-disabled post-merge gating before git deps load.

title: pan-1696: roles/flywheel.md defines scope semantics for the orchestrator
description:
In roles/flywheel.md (scope-contract paragraph at line ~50, inventory step at line ~83): define both scope values where the run-configuration 'Scope:' line is interpreted. pan-only keeps the current PAN-issue inventory verbatim. all-tracked-projects: inventory ready/in-flight work for every project registered in projects.yaml, applying the same hard author/assignee filter per tracker (GitHub: the existing author/assignee gates verbatim; Linear: only issues assigned to the operator), with the same needs-design/needs-discussion exclusions. State explicitly that merge/UAT trains assemble per project independent of run scope — the orchestrator observes them but does not gate them.
- AC: roles/flywheel.md contains definitions of pan-only and all-tracked-projects at the point where the Scope: config line is interpreted, including the per-tracker author/assignee safety gates for multi-project inventory
- AC: The role prompt contains an explicit statement that merge/UAT trains assemble per project regardless of run scope
close_reason: Defined pan-only/all-tracked-projects Flywheel scope semantics and per-project merge/UAT train independence; prompt lint passes.

metadata.acJustification: Prompt-content bead: two criteria fully pin the required semantics; a third would restate them.

title: pan-1696: Settings scope select: discoverability, help text, accurate apply-timing copy
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx (flywheel config block at lines 508-572; the scope select at 554-566): first VERIFY the select renders without any expansion interaction — the flywheelConfig block at line 508 is gated on role.id === 'flywheel' (line 458), not on isExpanded (line 575 gates sub-roles only); add a test asserting the scope select is present without expanding anything. Add help text under the select — pan-only: 'Orchestrate only the Panopticon repo's issues'; all-tracked-projects: 'Inventory and adopt ready work across every registered project (same author/assignee safety rules)' — and note it is distinct from per-project merge-train enablement. Correct the blanket 'Changes apply on the next tick' copy (line ~571) so scope is stated to apply at the next run start or resume. Extend a settings/RolesPanel test asserting a save carrying roles.flywheel.scope='all-tracked-projects' is reflected by the subsequent settings GET (settings-api.ts:405-415 already validates the union).
- AC: The scope select renders in Settings → Roles without any expansion interaction, with plain-language help text describing both values (asserted by component test and verified in browser via isolated Playwright instance)
- AC: The panel copy states that scope applies at the next run start or resume
- AC: A settings save of roles.flywheel.scope='all-tracked-projects' persists, and the subsequent settings GET returns it (asserted by test)
close_reason: Made Flywheel scope setting discoverable with help/apply-timing copy, verified save/reload persistence in component and Playwright tests; typecheck and lint pass.

title: pan-1696: Persist and display the active run's effective scope
description:
Persist the scope value handed to the run prompt (src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-actions.ts lines 215-300, where start/resume assemble FlywheelLifecycleOptions from roleConfig) onto the run record (src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-run-state.ts — extend the record shape and serialization) and expose it in the run detail/status payload consumed by the dashboard. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelConversationPane.tsx (lines 34-90): display the ACTIVE run's persisted effective scope, falling back to the configured settings value when no run is live; when the configured scope differs from the active run's scope, make the mismatch visible; add an inline note or tooltip reading 'Scope changes apply at the next run start or resume.' Update flywheel-run-state.test.ts and the pane's tests.
- AC: Starting or resuming a flywheel run persists the effective scope on the run record and the run detail/status payload exposes it
- AC: The Flywheel page renders the active run's persisted scope (not the live settings value) while a run is live, with the apply-timing note visible and a configured-vs-run mismatch made visible
- AC: With no active run, the pane falls back to displaying the configured scope from settings
close_reason: Implemented in 1e7053d60: persisted Flywheel run scope at start/resume, exposed scope in run list/detail payloads, and updated Flywheel pane/tests to show active run scope with configured-scope mismatch.

title: pan-1696: UAT stack frontend URL honors per-project DNS domain
description:
In src/lib/cloister/uat-stack.ts (URL builder at lines 127-140 and its callers): resolve the generation's project via findProjectByPathSync(gen.projectRoot) (src/lib/projects.ts:292) and build the host as https://<folder>.<domain> where domain comes from the project's dns config (verify the exact location: dns lives on WorkspaceConfig, src/lib/projects.ts:32-52) with 'pan.localhost' fallback. Update tests/unit/lib/cloister/uat-stack.test.ts with a custom-domain case and a fallback case.
- AC: The frontend URL renders as <folder>.<domain> when the generation's project config defines a dns domain
- AC: The frontend URL falls back to <folder>.pan.localhost when no project domain is configured (existing behavior preserved)
close_reason: Implemented in e23a4c429: UAT stack frontend URLs use the generation project's workspace.dns.domain when no rendered Host() label exists, with pan.localhost fallback and tests for both paths.

title: pan-1696: Pipeline-sourced per-project ready set (review-status DB, no flywheel run)
description:
In src/lib/flywheel-merge-order.ts: (1) add interface MergeCandidate { issueId: string; title: string; pr?: number }. (2) Refactor the git core of computeMergeQueue (lines 163-238) into computeMergeQueueFromCandidates(candidates, projectRoot, options) with identical behavior (eligibility gate via options.eligibility defaulting to reviewRecordEligibility, branch-existence check, file-footprint conflict graph, orderMergeCandidates, batchGroup) minus the MERGE_GATE_VERBS verb filter. Keep the FlywheelPipelineItem-based computeMergeQueue as a thin wrapper for now (deleted in remove-legacy-routes if orphaned). (3) Add listEligibleCandidatesByProject(opts?: { titleFor?: (issueId) => string | undefined }) returning Map<projectKey, { projectKey, projectRoot, candidates }>: getAllReviewStatusesFromDb() (src/lib/database/review-status-db.ts:235) -> keep records where mergeGateEligibility(s).eligible && s.deaconIgnored !== true (the readyForMerge boolean can be stale; the predicate is authoritative per PAN-1759) -> resolve each issue's project via resolveProjectFromIssueSync (src/lib/projects.ts:351), dropping issues that resolve to no project -> candidates with title = titleFor(issueId) ?? issueId and pr = s.prNumber. Adapt tests/unit/lib/flywheel-merge-order.test.ts to candidate inputs, preserving every ordering/conflict assertion, and add cases for the grouping/eligibility/title-fallback behaviors.
- AC: computeMergeQueueFromCandidates produces the same conflict-aware ordering, conflictsWith sets, and batchGroup values as the previous implementation for equivalent inputs (adapted tests preserve all ordering assertions)
- AC: listEligibleCandidatesByProject returns only records passing mergeGateEligibility with deaconIgnored !== true — review-not-passed, test-failed, verification-failed, already-merged, and deacon-ignored records are excluded even when their readyForMerge bit is stale
- AC: Given eligible records spanning two projects, each project's group contains only its own issues, and issues resolving to no registered project are dropped
- AC: Candidates carry title from the injected titleFor resolver, fall back to the issue ID when it returns undefined, and the call chain never reads flywheel run state or activePipeline
close_reason: Implemented in 32ffc22e5: added MergeCandidate and computeMergeQueueFromCandidates, kept computeMergeQueue as a Flywheel verb-filter wrapper, and added listEligibleCandidatesByProject from review-status DB with project grouping and eligibility/deaconIgnored filtering.

title: pan-1696: Move flag to merge_train.enabled with legacy fallback read
description:
In src/lib/database/app-settings.ts:77-150: add MERGE_TRAIN_ENABLED_KEY = 'merge_train.enabled'; rename the old constant to LEGACY_FLYWHEEL_MERGE_TRAIN_ENABLED_KEY. isMergeTrainEnabled(): read the new key; when null, read the legacy key; default false. setMergeTrainEnabled(): write the new key only. The flywheel config GET/POST payload (src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts:221-250) keeps exposing merge_train_enabled, now backed by the renamed helpers. Add unit tests covering new-key read, legacy fallback (ON value stored only under the old key), and write target. Before/after snippet in PRD work item 1.
- AC: isMergeTrainEnabled() returns the value stored under merge_train.enabled when that key exists, and falls back to flywheel.merge_train_enabled when the new key is absent (unit test covers an ON value stored only under the old key)
- AC: setMergeTrainEnabled(true) persists 'true' under merge_train.enabled, leaves the legacy key untouched, and the flywheel config GET/POST endpoints round-trip the toggle
- AC: A grep for the legacy key string returns no hits outside the fallback site, its constant, and tests
close_reason: Moved merge-train setting to merge_train.enabled with legacy fallback read, route round-trip coverage, and legacy-key grep cleanup.

title: pan-1807: pan review pending prints 'Unsubmitted handoff work' section
description:
In src/cli/commands/pending.ts (pan review pending, registered at src/cli/index.ts:325-328), call detectUnsubmittedHandoffWork() in the DEFAULT mode only — never in --ready mode, whose output feeds the flywheel tick (PAN-1735) and must stay byte-stable (hazard H6). Print the section after the pending-reviews output AND on the 'No pending reviews' early-return path: header 'Unsubmitted handoff work', then one block per row showing the issue id (chalk.yellow), '<n> commit(s) pushed on <branch>, no PR/review submitted', the workspace path, the conversation name with status and date, and 'Action: pan done <ISSUE>' (layout spec: W6 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1807.md). Print nothing extra when the detection list is empty. No new CLI flags, so the skills lint is unaffected; read skills/pan-review/SKILL.md and update it ONLY if its text contradicts the new output. Tests in src/cli/commands/__tests__/pending-handoff-section.test.ts mocking the detection module.
- AC: With one mocked detection row, pan review pending renders the section with issue id, ahead-count, branch, workspace, conversation, and the 'pan done <ISSUE>' action line — including when there are zero pending reviews
- AC: With an empty detection result, pan review pending prints default-mode output unchanged from today (no header, no blank section)
- AC: pan review pending --ready prints byte-identical output to current behavior and performs zero detection calls (mock asserts no invocations)

title: pan-1807: Async collectors + detectUnsubmittedHandoffWork()
description:
In src/lib/conversations/handoff-submission.ts add detectUnsubmittedHandoffWork(opts?: { sinceDays?: number }) returning Promise<UnsubmittedHandoffWork[]> (default sinceDays 7). Candidates: listConversations() (src/lib/database/conversations-db.ts:166) filtered to handoffDocPath !== null, createdAt within sinceDays, and cwd parseable by parseWorkspaceFromCwd imported from handoff-contract.ts (shared parser — do not duplicate the regex); dedupe to one candidate per workspace keeping the newest conversation. Per candidate collect with execAsync ONLY — hazard H4: this lib may later be imported by the dashboard server and execSync freezes the event loop (PAN-70). Collect: current branch via git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD in the workspace (skip candidate unless it matches feature/*); merge target via git rev-parse --abbrev-ref origin/HEAD mapped to origin/<name>, falling back to origin/main; aheadOfTargetCount via git rev-list --count <target>..origin/<branch>, skipping the candidate when origin/<branch> does not exist (never pushed — NonGoal); reviewStatus via getReviewStatusSync (src/lib/review-status.ts); completionMarkerExists via existsSync(join(AGENTS_DIR, 'agent-' + issueId.toLowerCase(), 'completed')); agentRunning via listRunningAgentsSync() computed ONCE outside the per-candidate loop; prExistsForBranch via gh pr list --head <branch> --state all --json number --limit 1 run in the workspace with a 5-second timeout (pattern: src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts:185), try/catch yielding null; trackerIssueClosed via the GitHub issue-state lookup pattern used by done.ts (resolveGitHubIssueSync), try/catch yielding null. Compose results through evaluateHandoffCandidate. A failing workspace logs console.warn and is skipped — the function never throws for one bad candidate. Tests: vi.mock the conversations-db module to verify the sinceDays filter, the non-workspace-cwd filter, and per-workspace dedupe; mock execAsync rather than racing real timeouts (hazard H7 — use vi.useFakeTimers if any real timer is unavoidable, per the repo fake-timers rule).
- AC: detectUnsubmittedHandoffWork returns only candidates whose conversations have handoffDocPath set, fall within sinceDays, and whose cwd parses to a feature workspace — verified with a mocked listConversations
- AC: Given two handoff conversations in the same workspace, detection produces one candidate row carrying the newest conversation's name and createdAt
- AC: A candidate whose git probe fails, whose branch is not feature/*, or whose origin/<branch> is missing is skipped with a console.warn and does not abort the remaining candidates
- AC: handoff-submission.ts contains zero execSync occurrences (grep-verified), and a gh/tracker lookup failure produces null candidate fields rather than a thrown error (test asserts both)

title: pan-1807: Append completion contract to handoff briefs in runForkPipeline
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts runForkPipeline (declared ~:3861), after the brief string is final (after the forkMode handoff/else branches that assign `summary`, ~:3976) and BEFORE updateConversationForkFallbackReason/injectForkSummary, add: if (forkMode === 'handoff') { const contract = buildHandoffCompletionContract(conv.cwd); if (contract) summary += contract; }. Key on the REQUESTED forkMode, not effectiveForkMode, so handoff-to-summary fallback briefs also carry the contract (decision D3 in .pan/continue.json). The contract must ride the SAME string through injectForkSummary — never a second delivery, because PAN-1624 deliver-and-confirm may redeliver the one string (hazard H1). This single append point covers all four brief sources: external-authored docs, source-authored docs, reused docs via readExistingHandoffDoc, and fallback summaries. Do not modify injectForkSummary. Verify behavior with a test near the existing conversations route tests (src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/) or, if runForkPipeline is untestable in isolation there, by unit-testing the appended-string construction through the contract-helper plus a typecheck-verified wiring review.
- AC: A handoff fork whose successor cwd is inside workspaces/feature-<x> produces an injected brief that ends with the completion contract naming pan done <X>
- AC: A handoff fork into a non-workspace cwd produces a brief identical to current behavior (no contract), and summary/plain forks render without any contract
- AC: A handoff that falls back to summary mode (authoring failure) in a feature-workspace cwd still delivers the contract footer inside the same single injected string

title: pan-1807: Pure verdict logic evaluateHandoffCandidate + types
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/handoff-submission.ts with the types HandoffWorkCandidate and UnsubmittedHandoffWork (exact field lists in .pan/drafts/PAN-1807.md W4) and the PURE function evaluateHandoffCandidate(c) returning the UnsubmittedHandoffWork row or null. Flag iff aheadOfTargetCount >= 1 AND none of these suppress: (a) c.reviewStatus.reviewStatus is one of pending/reviewing/passed, OR c.reviewStatus.mergeStatus === 'merged', OR c.reviewStatus.prUrl is non-empty; (b) c.completionMarkerExists; (c) c.agentRunning; (d) c.prExistsForBranch === true; (e) c.trackerIssueClosed === true. Best-effort nulls (prExistsForBranch or trackerIssueClosed === null) NEVER suppress — decision D6 in .pan/continue.json: a false positive in a CLI list is cheaper than the silent miss this issue exists to fix. No I/O imports in this bead; the function is a pure mapping unit-tested without git/DB/network in src/lib/conversations/__tests__/handoff-submission.test.ts.
- AC: evaluateHandoffCandidate returns the UnsubmittedHandoffWork row for a candidate with aheadOfTargetCount>=1 and all suppression inputs falsy or null
- AC: Each suppression input — pipeline review status, merged status, non-empty prUrl, completion marker, running agent, prExistsForBranch=true, trackerIssueClosed=true — independently produces a null return (one test per rule)
- AC: aheadOfTargetCount === 0 returns null, and prExistsForBranch=null / trackerIssueClosed=null do NOT suppress an otherwise-flagged candidate

title: pan-1807: pan done resolves the canonical workspace when agent state is absent
description:
In src/cli/commands/done.ts add a single workspace resolver used by all three resolution sites — preflight (:261-263), rebase (:330-334), review-artifact creation (:409-415): prefer getAgentStateSync(agentId)?.workspace when it exists on disk; otherwise resolve the project via resolveProjectFromIssueSync (src/lib/projects.ts:351 — VERIFY the actual project-path field name on its return type before wiring, hazard H5) and use join(<project path>, 'workspaces', 'feature-' + issueId.toLowerCase()) if existsSync (path convention precedent: src/cli/commands/inspect.ts:64, doctor.ts:444, review-spawn-reviewer.ts:44). Keep the `if (existingState)` guard at :436 — when state is absent the state mutation is skipped; the completion-marker write at :449 already mkdirSync's the agent dir, and checkCompletionMarkers (src/lib/cloister/service.ts:1047) derives the issue from the dir name, so the downstream pipeline proceeds without state.json. Update the :413 error message to name both missing sources: no agent state and no workspaces/feature-<issue> directory. Add tests in src/cli/commands/__tests__/done-workspace-fallback.test.ts.
- AC: With agent state present, the resolver returns the state.json workspace (existing behavior preserved, verified by test)
- AC: With no agent state and an existing workspaces/feature-<issue> directory, pan done proceeds through preflight, rebase, and review-artifact creation against that directory and exits 0 on a clean tree
- AC: With no agent state and no canonical workspace directory, pan done exits non-zero with the updated error message naming both missing sources
- AC: A test suite at src/cli/commands/__tests__/done-workspace-fallback.test.ts covers ac1-ac3 and passes under npm test

title: pan-1807: Pure helper buildHandoffCompletionContract(cwd) + workspace-path parser
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/handoff-contract.ts exporting (1) parseWorkspaceFromCwd(cwd) returning { issueId, workspacePath } or null — matches the /workspaces/feature-<segment>/ pattern anywhere in cwd (regex precedent: src/cli/commands/resources.ts:159) and parses the segment with parseIssueIdFromTextSync from src/lib/resource-utils.ts; and (2) buildHandoffCompletionContract(cwd) returning null when the parser returns null, otherwise a Markdown footer starting with a horizontal rule and a '## Completion contract' heading that states: pushing commits is NOT completion; run pan done <ISSUE> (uppercase) from the workspace root (it rebases, pushes, creates the PR/review artifacts, and enters the review pipeline); if pan done fails, report the exact error verbatim in the final message instead of stopping silently; if the work is intentionally not for submission, say so explicitly in the final message. Exact content spec: Decision rule 8 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1807.md. Pure string logic — no git, no fs, no DB imports. Unit tests in src/lib/conversations/__tests__/handoff-contract.test.ts.
- AC: Given cwd /home/u/Projects/x/workspaces/feature-pan-1800, buildHandoffCompletionContract returns a footer containing 'pan done PAN-1800' and '## Completion contract'
- AC: Given a cwd that is a subdirectory of a workspace (.../workspaces/feature-pan-1800/src/lib), the helper still returns the PAN-1800 contract
- AC: Given a non-workspace cwd (/home/u/Projects/x, /tmp/foo) or an unparseable segment (workspaces/feature-notanissue), the helper returns null
- AC: A unit suite at src/lib/conversations/__tests__/handoff-contract.test.ts covers ac1-ac3 and passes under npm test

title: pan-1775: Seed session-tree feature candidates from active remote agent states
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts fetchProjectSessionTree() (lines ~471-542): seed feature candidates from listActiveRemoteAgentStates() (exported by src/lib/remote/remote-agents.ts:82) in addition to the existing workspaces/feature-* directory scan (lines 503-510). Use the dynamic-import + try/catch guard pattern from src/dashboard/server/services/resource-discovery.ts:507-521 (import '../../../lib/remote/remote-agents.js' adjusted for this file's relative depth; on import failure, skip remote seeding silently so the tree still returns workspace-derived features). For each RemoteAgentState ({ issueId, vmName, model, status, location }): (1) skip if its issueId does not resolve to the requested project — use resolveProjectFromIssue from src/lib/projects.ts and compare the resolved project's key with project.key; (2) skip if a candidate with the same issueLower already exists from the workspace scan (dedupe — e.g. PAN-1762 had both a local workspace and a remote agent); (3) otherwise append { name: `feature-${issueLower}`, issueLower, issueId } and let the existing per-candidate loop process it. The existing loop already handles remote-only candidates: its hasAgent check passes because ~/.panopticon/agents/agent-<issueLower>/ contains remote-state.json, and collectSessionTreeNodes synthesizes the remote SessionNode (remote: { provider: 'fly.io', vmName } at projects.ts:269) even when the workspacePath does not exist on disk. Structural requirement: the candidate-processing loop currently lives inside `if (await pathExists(workspacesDir))` (line 501) — restructure minimally so remote-derived candidates are processed even when the workspaces directory is absent. Per hazard H4, the unit test must prove resolveJsonlPath and getSessionTreeWorkspacePath tolerate the missing workspace directory. Add the test to the existing suite tests/unit/dashboard/server/routes/projects.test.ts (created by commit 441eeecbc), following its existing mocking patterns.
- AC: Given an active remote-state.json (location 'remote', status 'running') for an issue with NO workspaces/feature-<issue> directory, fetchProjectSessionTree returns a feature entry for that issue whose sessions include a work row carrying remote.provider 'fly.io' and the state's vmName
- AC: An issue discovered by both the workspace scan and remote seeding produces exactly one feature entry (dedupe by issueLower preserves the workspace-scan candidate)
- AC: The returned tree excludes remote agents whose issueId resolves to a different project than the requested projectKey
- AC: When the remote-agents module import fails, fetchProjectSessionTree falls back to workspace-derived features only and does not throw
- AC: A new unit test in tests/unit/dashboard/server/routes/projects.test.ts asserts the no-local-workspace remote case returns the synthesized remote session row, proving the missing-workspacePath path does not throw

title: pan-1775: Untrack workspace .pan/continue.json and .pan/spec.vbrief.json from the feature branch
description:
From the workspace root (/home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1775), run `git rm --cached .pan/continue.json .pan/spec.vbrief.json` and commit as a new commit (suggested message: 'fix(repo): untrack workspace planning working copies — review blocker, PAN-1775'). This resolves the cycle-3 blocking finding (tracked .pan/continue.json carrying unrelated PAN-1788 state) and the identical inherited violation on .pan/spec.vbrief.json (tracked since the merge-base, also PAN-1073-violating). Do NOT delete either file from disk — both are live workspace runtime state for this issue. Do NOT edit .gitignore: both paths are already ignored (.gitignore lines 59-60 and 120/123). Do NOT rewrite history (no amend, no rebase of pushed commits). Known pitfall: while the files are tracked, `git check-ignore` reports them as NOT ignored — gitignore patterns do not apply to tracked files; the check passes only after untracking. Watch hazard H2: a later rebase against main may raise a modify/delete conflict on these paths because origin/main does not track them — resolve in favor of deletion from the index; the on-disk files are untouched.
- AC: Given the fix commit, `git ls-files -- .pan/continue.json .pan/spec.vbrief.json` run at the workspace root returns no output (neither path remains tracked on feature/pan-1775)
- AC: Both files remain present on disk with their PAN-1775 content, and `git check-ignore .pan/continue.json .pan/spec.vbrief.json` exits 0 after untracking (git ignores both paths going forward)
- AC: The removal lands as a new commit appended to feature/pan-1775 and preserves all prior branch history (no amend, no rebase of pushed commits)
close_reason: Untracked .pan/continue.json and .pan/spec.vbrief.json during rebase onto origin/main; verified not tracked, gitignored, and preserved on disk.

title: pan-1799: End-to-end integration coverage for the boot-death and ghost-kickoff flows
description:
Extend tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts (existing harness mocks tmux via exactPaneTarget/sessionExists mocks — follow the established pattern at lines 216-512) with the full boot-death scenario: spawn a codex work-tui agent whose mocked session disappears before readiness; assert spawnAgent throws AgentBootError, the persisted state is 'stopped', the boot-failure.log write was attempted with the mocked pane tail, and the dead-session bail consumed only ~1 poll interval of fake time (not the full 60s of two timeout rounds). Re-assert the alive-but-undelivered ghost lifecycle suite (lines 357-512) is green. ALL timing via vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync() — repo rule, no real delays, no maxForks changes.
- AC: Integration test: when a codex work-tui spawn's session disappears before readiness, spawnAgent throws AgentBootError, persists state 'stopped', and records the mocked pane tail in the boot diagnostic
- AC: Integration test: the dead-session path returns within roughly one poll interval of fake time, not the full two 30s readiness rounds
- AC: The pre-existing ghost-lifecycle suite (kickoff failure with live session, resume redelivery) passes unchanged, and every new test covers its timing with fake timers exclusively

title: pan-1799: pan start reports boot outcomes truthfully and terminates deterministically
description:
In src/cli/commands/start.ts issueCommand: (1) AgentBootError now propagates to the existing catch (line ~1170) which already does spinner.fail + process.exit(1) — ensure the boot diagnostic (pane tail) is printed in full, not just the error message's first line; (2) when spawnAgent returns a state with kickoffDelivered === false (alive-but-undelivered ghost), replace the unqualified '✔ Agent spawned' success block with spinner.warn plus an explicit note that the kickoff was not delivered and the deacon will redeliver on resume (mention pan logs <id> and pan tell <id>); (3) append explicit process.exit(0) as the final statement of the success path so undrained event-loop handles (fire-and-forget tracker HTTP, TLDR daemon exec, Effect runtime) can never hang the CLI after the summary — precedent: close.ts:217, health.ts:175, dev.ts:678. Live UAT (test role): pan start a scratch codex agent (gpt-5.5) — the agent survives at least 2 minutes with the kickoff pointer visible in the TUI and pan start exits 0 within seconds of printing the summary; a forced mid-boot session kill makes pan start exit 1 with the captured diagnostic.
- AC: When the spawned session dies during boot, pan start prints the boot diagnostic and exits with code 1 — no '✔ Agent spawned' success line appears
- AC: When the session is alive but kickoff delivery failed, pan start prints an explicit kickoff-failure warning naming the deacon redelivery path instead of the unqualified success block
- AC: On success, the pan start process exits with code 0 within seconds of printing the agent summary (explicit process.exit(0))
- AC: Live UAT: a codex work agent spawned via pan start survives at least 2 minutes with the kickoff visible in its TUI and pan start exits promptly; killing the session mid-boot produces exit code 1 with the captured pane diagnostic

title: pan-1799: Capture boot-death diagnostics via a bounded tmux remain-on-exit window
description:
In spawnAgent (src/lib/agents.ts:3637-3649): create the work-agent session with tmux remain-on-exit ON (use setOption from src/lib/tmux.ts right after createSession — planning sessions already use remain-on-exit, see spawn-planning-session.ts for the pattern). After readiness/delivery succeeds, set remain-on-exit OFF so post-boot deaths still vanish and the deacon orphan patrol semantics are unchanged (NFR-3, hazard H2). In the boot-death branch (spawn-boot-contract's 'boot-died' path), before killing anything: capturePane the dead pane's last ~60 lines, write them to <agentDir>/boot-failure.log, embed the last ~10 lines in the AgentBootError diagnostic, then killSession to remove the remained session. If capture fails (pane truly gone), proceed with a 'no pane output captured' diagnostic — capture is best-effort and must never block the throw. Tests with fake timers: dead-pane capture writes the log and the error contains the tail; success path flips remain-on-exit off and writes no log.
- AC: spawnAgent creates work-agent sessions with remain-on-exit on and applies remain-on-exit off after kickoff delivery succeeds
- AC: On boot death, spawnAgent persists the dead pane's captured tail to ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/boot-failure.log, records the tail in the AgentBootError message, and closes the remained session
- AC: When pane capture fails, spawnAgent still throws AgentBootError with a 'no pane output captured' diagnostic and still kills the remained session
- AC: Given a successful boot, spawnAgent produces no boot-failure.log and the session ends up with remain-on-exit off, so later deaths surface to the deacon exactly as before

title: pan-1799: spawnAgent boot-outcome contract: throw AgentBootError on dead session, preserve ghost lifecycle when alive
description:
In src/lib/agents.ts: define an exported AgentBootError class (fields: agentId, harness, phase: 'boot-died' | 'kickoff-failed', diagnostic?: string). In spawnAgent's kickoff-failure paths (the pi catch at lines 3667-3673 and the delivery-failed branch at lines 3684-3687), after recordKickoffDeliveryFailure resolves, check sessionExists(agentId): if the session is GONE, mark the agent state stopped (markAgentStoppedState + save) and throw AgentBootError(phase 'boot-died'); if the session is ALIVE, keep the existing behavior exactly (return state with status 'running', kickoffDelivered false — the deacon ghost-lifecycle contract locked by tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts:357-512). Also check sessionExists immediately before the markAgentRunning(state) at line 3691 so a session that died after a seemingly-ok delivery is caught. State must be stopped BEFORE the throw so the dashboard start route (which calls spawnAgent) never leaves a phantom 'running' record. Unit/integration tests with fake timers for both branches.
- AC: When the tmux session is dead after kickoff delivery fails, spawnAgent persists agent state status 'stopped' and throws AgentBootError carrying agentId, harness, and phase 'boot-died'
- AC: When kickoff delivery fails but the session is alive, spawnAgent returns state with status 'running' and kickoffDelivered false — existing ghost-lifecycle tests (agent-spawning.test.ts:357-512) pass unchanged
- AC: When the session dies after delivery succeeded but before markAgentRunning, spawnAgent throws AgentBootError instead of persisting a 'running' state
- AC: The dashboard start-agent route returns a non-2xx response with the AgentBootError message when spawnAgent throws, and the persisted agent state reads 'stopped'

title: pan-1799: Lazy codex thread-id resolution with write-back for work-tui agents
description:
In src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts: add resolveCodexThreadId(agentId): string | null — read the codex-thread-id file (threadIdPath, codex.ts:56); when absent or empty, scan the per-agent rollout directory <agentDir>/codex-home/sessions/ recursively for the newest rollout-*.jsonl by mtime (reuse the traversal pattern of collectRollouts, codex.ts:585-621), extract the id via extractThreadIdFromRollout (codex.ts:310), write it back with writeThreadId (codex.ts:76), and return it; return null when no rollout exists (agent never booted). Wire it into: (1) getLatestSessionIdSync fallback #4 in src/lib/agents.ts:2387-2396 (replace the direct file read), and (2) readCodexThreadId in src/lib/memory/transcript-source.ts:350-357 (so CodexTranscriptSource.resolveAgentTranscript stops returning null for work-tui agents whose spawn never captured a thread-id). Do NOT add any wait/poll at spawn time — resolution is strictly on-demand (decision D4). Unit tests: file present, file absent + rollout present (write-back happens), neither present (null); transcript-source test that a running codex work agent with a rollout but no thread-id file produces a TranscriptEntry.
- AC: resolveCodexThreadId returns the saved codex-thread-id when the file exists, without touching the rollout directory
- AC: With no codex-thread-id file but an existing rollout, resolveCodexThreadId extracts the id from the newest rollout, persists it to codex-thread-id, and returns it; with neither, it returns null
- AC: getLatestSessionIdSync resolves a session id for a work-tui codex agent whose spawn never wrote codex-thread-id
- AC: CodexTranscriptSource.resolveAgentTranscript returns a TranscriptEntry (not null) for a running codex work agent with a rollout but no pre-existing codex-thread-id file

title: pan-1799: Detect dead tmux sessions early in readiness waits and kickoff delivery
description:
In src/lib/agents.ts: (1) add a sessionExists(agentId) check to each iteration of waitForReadySignal (lines 783-804) so it returns false within one poll interval when the session is gone — mirroring the existing bail in waitForCodexTuiReady at line 262; (2) in deliverInitialPromptWithRetry (lines 1614-1671), when readiness fails because the session no longer exists, set lastFailure to 'session-died' (distinct from 'ready-signal-timeout') and break out of the retry loop instead of attempting a second delivery to a dead session. waitForPromptReady (line 288) is the shared dispatch — thread a way to distinguish 'not ready' from 'session gone' (e.g. return 'ready' | 'timeout' | 'died'), updating the three other waitForPromptReady call sites (agents.ts:3337, 4290, 4885) to treat 'died' as not-ready exactly as before. Tests: extend tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts with fake timers (vi.useFakeTimers + advanceTimersByTimeAsync) — never real delays.
- AC: Given a tmux session that no longer exists, waitForReadySignal returns false within one poll interval (~1s of fake time) instead of polling the full 30s timeout
- AC: When the session is gone, deliverInitialPromptWithRetry returns a failure containing 'session-died' and performs no second delivery attempt
- AC: A live-but-not-ready session still produces 'ready-signal-timeout' after 2 attempts — existing kickoff-timeout tests in tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts pass unchanged

title: pan-1798: Regression-lock exit-empty off and the clean-founding command line with unit tests
description:
Per PRD WI-3, add tests/lib/tmux-managed-config.test.ts locking the b7e4ab885 behaviors that currently have zero test coverage: (1) with PANOPTICON_HOME pointed at a temp dir and child_process mocked, drive the managed-config ensure path and assert the written panopticon.tmux.conf contains the line 'set -g exit-empty off'; (2) assert the clean-founding argv is systemd-run --user --scope --collect --unit=panopticon-tmux-<timestamp> --quiet tmux -L panopticon -f <managed conf path> start-server (timestamp matched by pattern) and contains no launcher path or agent/conversation-specific strings. Test discipline per PAN-1794: all tmux interaction mocked; no test may target the live 'panopticon' socket; no real timers.

title: pan-1798: Warn loudly when the managed tmux server's cgroup is a tmux-spawn scope
description:
Per PRD WI-2, in src/lib/tmux.ts: (1) add exported pure function cgroupIndicatesSpawnScope(cgroupContent: string): boolean — true iff any line of /proc/<pid>/cgroup content has a path whose final component matches /^tmux-spawn-.*\.scope$/ (pure-function test seam, same pattern as paneTreeHasHarnessProcess); (2) add getManagedServerPid helper using `tmux -L panopticon display-message -p '#{pid}'` (verified to return the server PID with zero sessions), returning null on any failure; (3) wire a guard step into ensurePanopticonTmuxServer/Sync after the server is confirmed alive: resolve PID, read /proc/<pid>/cgroup, and when cgroupIndicatesSpawnScope is true emit a console.error prefixed '[tmux-founder-guard]' naming the offending scope unit and the server PID; warn at most once per server PID per process (module-level last-warned PID); silent no-op when /proc read fails (non-Linux); NEVER restart, signal, or migrate the server. Tests in tests/lib/tmux-founder-guard.test.ts: pure-function cases plus mocked-exec integration cases.
- AC: cgroupIndicatesSpawnScope returns true for cgroup content ending in tmux-spawn-<uuid>.scope and false for panopticon-tmux-*.scope, app.slice, and user session scope paths
- AC: Given an ensured live server whose /proc/<pid>/cgroup names a tmux-spawn-*.scope, the ensure helper emits a console.error containing '[tmux-founder-guard]', the scope unit name, and the server PID
- AC: The guard emits the warning at most once per server PID per process; after a re-founding produces a new server PID inside a spawn scope, the guard emits the warning again
- AC: When /proc/<pid>/cgroup is unreadable or the PID cannot be resolved, the guard returns silently without throwing and issues no kill/restart/migration command (asserted via exec mock receiving no such invocation)

title: pan-1798: De-latch managed-server founding into an ensurePanopticonTmuxServer preflight on every new-session
description:
In src/lib/tmux.ts, per PRD WI-1: (1) add foundManagedServerAsync mirroring foundManagedServerSync (tmux.ts:114-127) using execFileAsync, and make both log a console.warn '[tmux-server] systemd-run unavailable — tmux server founded without scope isolation' on the plain start-server fallback; (2) add isManagedServerAliveAsync mirroring isManagedServerAliveSync (tmux.ts:92-99); (3) export ensurePanopticonTmuxServer(): Promise<void> and ensurePanopticonTmuxServerSync(): no-op unless getTmuxConfigMode() === 'managed'; check aliveness; found cleanly if dead; assert aliveness after founding and console.error on failure (no retries — PRD D5); (4) in tmuxExecAsync (tmux.ts:239) and tmuxExecSync (tmux.ts:245), after ensureTmuxContextPrepared*, call the matching ensure variant when mode === 'managed' && args[0] === 'new-session'; (5) keep the tmuxContextPrepared latch for config-file writing only — founding must no longer be guarded by it (remove or delegate the founding block inside reloadManagedTmuxConfigSync/Async at tmux.ts:136-138,150-154). Dashboard boot (src/dashboard/server/main.ts:80 ensureManagedTmuxContextOnce) must still found at startup through the new helper. Tests in tests/lib/tmux-ensure-server.test.ts with vi.mock('child_process') and a temp PANOPTICON_HOME; never target the live 'panopticon' socket.
- AC: Given managed mode and a dead managed socket, when tmuxExecAsync runs a new-session command, it first founds the server via systemd-run --user --scope --collect --unit=panopticon-tmux-<unique> --quiet tmux -L panopticon -f <conf> start-server (mock-asserted argv) and then executes the new-session
- AC: Given a live managed server, the new-session preflight performs no founding — neither systemd-run nor start-server is invoked beyond the single aliveness probe
- AC: Given the tmux server dies after the process has already prepared the tmux context once, a subsequent new-session re-founds the server cleanly (regression test for the once-per-process tmuxContextPrepared latch)
- AC: Given systemd-run fails or is absent, founding falls back to plain tmux start-server and emits a console warning stating scope isolation is unavailable
- AC: Given a live managed server, when tmuxExecAsync runs a non-session-creating command (list-sessions, capture-pane), it produces no aliveness probe subprocess; the async preflight founds via execFileAsync so no new blocking execFileSync is reachable from the async path

title: pan-1805: End-to-end verification of the codex conversation view plus branch hygiene
description:
Prove the fix end-to-end and clean the branch for review. (1) With the dashboard rebuilt (npm run build; Node 22 dist only) and a codex work agent available (agent-pan-1803 if still running, else any codex agent with a rollout on disk), confirm the command-deck session payload carries hasJsonl: true / a jsonlPath pointing at the rollout, and the conversation view renders the transcript instead of 'No conversation data available'. If no live codex agent exists, verify via the route tests plus a manual API call against an agent dir fixture. (2) Run the full quality gates. title: pan-1805: Conversations /:name/messages agent-name fallback resolves codex rollouts via the shared resolver

In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, the agent-name fallback used by GET /api/conversations/:name/messages was claude-only; codex-harness agents must resolve their rollout there too. The route's previously local resolveCodexSessionFile duplicate is replaced by importing the shared resolveCodexRolloutPath from ./jsonl-resolver.js, and codex rollouts are parsed with the existing parseCodexConversationMessages from ../services/codex-conversation-parser.js. title: pan-1805: resolveJsonlPath dispatches on agent harness and resolves codex rollouts
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/jsonl-resolver.ts, resolveJsonlPath must dispatch on the harness recorded in the agent's state.json (resolveAgentHarness) BEFORE attempting claude-code resolution. Codex agents resolve via resolveCodexRolloutPath: fast path through the persisted ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/codex-thread-id (findRolloutPath), lazy fallback through findLatestRollout over codex-home/sessions (the PAN-1690 shape). Export resolveCodexRolloutPath for reuse by the conversations route. Already implemented (jsonl-resolver.ts ~lines 170-211, tests in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/jsonl-resolver.test.ts) — verify against the current tree and repair any drift.

title: pan-1805: spawnAgent captures the codex thread-id post-kickoff without blocking spawn
description:
In src/lib/agents.ts spawnAgent, after kickoff delivery for a codex-harness work agent, run a non-blocking background task (void async IIFE) that calls waitForCodexRollout on the agent's codex-home (120s budget), extracts the thread-id via extractThreadIdFromRollout, and persists it with writeThreadId. Codex work-tui writes its rollout only after the first prompt, which is why this polls post-kickoff instead of at spawn. spawnRun's pre-existing exec-only capture (agents.ts ~3304) stays untouched. Already implemented (agents.ts ~3699) — verify against the current tree and repair any drift.
- AC: After codex work-agent kickoff, spawnAgent persists the captured thread-id to ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/codex-thread-id via writeThreadId once the rollout appears
- AC: The capture runs as a non-blocking background task: spawnAgent returns without waiting for it, and a rollout that never appears (timeout) produces no spawn failure
- AC: spawnRun preserves its existing exec-specialist thread-id capture behavior unchanged (gating at agents.ts ~3304 untouched)

title: pan-1805: findLatestRollout/waitForCodexRollout skip subagent (guardian) rollouts
description:
In src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts, findLatestRollout must prefer user-thread rollouts over codex-internal subagent rollouts (e.g. the guardian approval supervisor), discriminated by the session_meta first-line record's payload.thread_source === 'subagent'. waitForCodexRollout reuses the same preference so spawn-time thread-id capture also picks the user thread. Already implemented (codex.ts ~lines 264-370, tests in src/lib/runtimes/__tests__/codex.test.ts) — verify against the current tree and repair any drift from main syncs.
- AC: Given a codex-home containing a user-thread rollout and a NEWER subagent rollout, findLatestRollout returns the user-thread rollout (covered by a test in src/lib/runtimes/__tests__/codex.test.ts)
- AC: When ALL rollouts are subagent threads, findLatestRollout falls back to returning the newest rollout rather than null
- AC: Given a rollout whose session_meta lacks thread_source (older codex versions), findLatestRollout returns it as a user-thread candidate

title: pan-1808: Implement issue
description:
## Evidence (2026-06-12)

After the PAN-1803 work agent ran `npm test` locally in its workspace at 11:35, a leftover session appeared on the shared agent socket:

```
$ tmux -L panopticon list-sessions
agent-pan-resume-redeliver: 1 windows (created Fri Jun 12 11:35:55 2026)
```

The pane contains a live interactive `claude --resume` session picker ("No sessions match …"). The name matches the kickoff-redelivery tests in `tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts` ("resumeAgent re-delivers the original kickoff when kickoffDelivered is false", etc.).

## Why it matters

- Leaked interactive claude processes accumulate RAM and API-session weight.
- Sessions named `agent-*` on the panopticon socket are picked up by resource discovery / patrols and can confuse orphan detection.
- Tests that touch the real shared socket can interfere with live agents (cf. PAN-1798's shared-server kill blast radius).

## Desired behavior

Integration tests must never create sessions on the shared `panopticon` socket: use a per-run throwaway socket name (e.g. `-L pan-test-$PID`) injected via the tmux lib, and kill the socket's server in `afterAll`. The spawned command in these tests should be a stub, not the real `claude` binary.

## Cleanup
The leaked session was killed manually after capture.

---
Flywheel-Run-Id: RUN-23
Flywheel-Filed-By: agent
- AC: Leaked interactive claude processes accumulate RAM and API-session weight.
- AC: Sessions named agent-* on the panopticon socket are picked up by resource discovery / patrols and can confuse orphan detection.
- AC: Tests that touch the real shared socket can interfere with live agents (cf. PAN-1798's shared-server kill blast radius).
close_reason: Implemented a validated tmux socket override, isolated agent-spawning tests onto a throwaway socket with a stub claude binary, added socket override regression tests, and verified typecheck/lint/npm test.

title: pan-1803: Implement issue
description:
**Architecture bug (keystone).** Codex work agents are launched via `codex exec` (one-shot: runs one turn, writes its rollout only on the first turn, then exits). The work-agent lifecycle expects a **persistent** session — the same model claude-code (interactive `claude`) and pi (long-lived `--mode rpc`) use — so when a codex agent finishes its turn and exits, the lifecycle marks it `orphaned: tmux session missing at boot` and the deacon treats it as dead. Net: codex work agents boot (after PAN-1799), run once, vanish, and never complete the bead loop or accept `pan tell` feedback.

**One-shot is a deliberately-rejected architecture** (it has cost hundreds of hours historically) — agents must be live interactive sessions. The fix is to make codex work agents follow the **claude-code work-agent pattern**, which Panopticon spent its lifetime getting right.

**Reference pattern (claude-code, the template):**
- `buildNonConversationCommand` in `src/lib/launcher-generator.ts` emits interactive `claude --permission-mode auto --model ...`, wrapped via `wrapWithSupervisor` (PTY supervisor). The session stays alive between turns.
- Prompt delivery: `agents.ts` waits for a readiness signal (`waitForReadySignal` → SessionStart hook ready.json) then delivers the kickoff via tmux/supervisor. `pan tell` delivers subsequent messages to the same live session.
- Lifecycle: a live tmux session IS the normal running state; completion = `pan done`, not process exit.
- Codex **conversations** already run persistently as TUI (`buildCodexCommand` codexMode='tui' → `codex -c project_doc_max_bytes=0` under the supervisor) — proof codex can do this.

**Required change (mirror claude-code):**
1. `buildCodexCommand` — for WORK agents, emit interactive codex (NOT `exec`): `codex -m <model> -s <workspace-write> -c approval_policy=never --skip-git-repo-check` wrapped with the PTY supervisor; the kickoff prompt is delivered post-readiness, NOT embedded inline. Keep `getCodexLauncherFields` but drop the hardcoded one-shot `codexMode:'exec'` for work (add a work-tui mode or equivalent). Conversations keep their existing tui path; exec mode may remain only if some non-work caller still needs it (audit callers).
2. `agents.ts` delivery — codex work agents must take the tmux/supervisor delivery path (today `prompt && resolvedHarness !== 'codex'` SKIPS codex because exec embedded the prompt). Add a codex-TUI readiness check (capture-pane for the codex prompt, analogous to `waitForPiTuiReady`) before delivering.
3. Keep `waitForCodexRollout`/thread-id capture for resume; verify resume uses the interactive (not exec) path too.
4. Lifecycle: confirm the orphan-at-boot path no longer fires once the session persists; if an explicit codex-readiness gate is needed, add it.

**Acceptance:** a `pan start <id> --model gpt-5.5 --harness codex` work agent (a) survives >3 min idle after finishing its kickoff turn (tmux session stays alive), (b) processes a `pan tell <id> "..."` message delivered after it goes idle, (c) closes a bead and continues to the next without the process exiting, (d) is NOT marked orphaned by the deacon while alive. Test against a scratch issue; do not rely on unit tests alone — this is a live-session behavior. typecheck + lint green; existing codex conversation tests stay green.

Refs: PAN-1799 (boot fixes — prerequisite, merged), PAN-1574 (codex first-class — introduced the exec mode), and the claude-code work-agent path as the reference implementation.

title: pan-1787: Graceful restart: 60s grace + Escape interrupt for TUI harnesses

Update src/lib/cloister/__tests__/specialist-harnesses-config.test.ts and specialist-harness-router.test.ts to the alias semantics (do not delete coverage — rewrite assertions).

title: pan-1787: Dashboard start route: validate, fail fast, forward explicit harness only

Do NOT resolve-and-forward an unset harness as if it were explicit — that would bypass role overrides and double-fire the built-in-default notice.

title: pan-1787: Fresh-session invariant: drop --resume across model/harness change
description:

Sessions with NO persisted origin metadata (pre-change) resume as today (recorded autoDecision). restartAgent already never passes resumeSessionId — leave it; add a regression test locking that. Tests: restart with newModel only / newHarness only / both ⇒ generated launcher contains no --resume; resume with unchanged model+harness keeps --resume <id>; resume with drifted model drops it; resume with missing origin metadata keeps --resume.

Round-trip tests in the existing settings-api suite: save with override persists the key; save with '' removes the key from YAML; GET on unset returns no override plus the built-in map.

title: pan-1787: Unified resolveHarness() in new src/lib/harness-resolve.ts
description:
Per PRD WI-2: create src/lib/harness-resolve.ts exporting `resolveHarness(input: { explicit?: RuntimeName; role?: Role; model: string }): Promise<RuntimeName>` with precedence (first defined wins): 1) explicit, 2) loadYamlConfig().config.roles[role]?.harness, 3) config.providerHarnesses[provider] where provider = getProviderForModelSync(model).name, 4) getBuiltInDefaultHarness(provider), 5) 'claude-code'. The winner ALWAYS passes through canUseHarnessSync(winner, model, await getProviderAuthMode(model)); blocked ⇒ 'claude-code'. If the winner is 'pi' or 'codex' and its binary is not installed, collapse to 'claude-code' with a console warning — implement an async availability helper using promisified exec `command -v <bin>` with a module-level cache (do NOT reuse doctor's execSync-based checkCommand; resolveHarness is dashboard-server-reachable and execSync is forbidden there). When tier 4 (built-in default) decides, emit a one-line notice once per provider per process: "harness <h> chosen by provider default — override in Settings → Providers". Write tests/unit/lib/harness-resolve.test.ts covering the full precedence matrix: explicit>role>provider-override>built-in; empty config + anthropic⇒claude-code, + openai⇒codex, + kimi/google/zai⇒pi; policy-blocked winner (explicit pi + anthropic model + subscription auth) collapses to claude-code; missing binary collapses to claude-code with warning. Mock the availability check and policy/auth lookups in tests.
- AC: resolveHarness returns the explicit harness when provided, else the role override, else the provider override, else the built-in default — the full precedence-matrix unit suite passes
- AC: With empty providerHarnesses and no role override, resolveHarness returns claude-code for an Anthropic model, codex for an OpenAI model, and pi for kimi/google/zai models
- AC: Every winner passes through canUseHarnessSync: explicit pi + Anthropic model + subscription auth returns claude-code; existing harness-policy tests pass unmodified
- AC: When the winning pi/codex binary is absent, resolveHarness returns claude-code and logs a warning, using only async exec (no execSync anywhere in the new module)
- AC: The first resolution that lands on a built-in default emits the one-line override-hint notice exactly once per provider per process
close_reason: Added unified resolveHarness with precedence, policy gate, async binary fallback, provider-default notice, and unit coverage.

title: pan-1787: Persist session-origin model+harness beside claudeSessionId

Unit test: after a spawn, runtime state contains sessionModel/sessionHarness matching the spawn's resolved values; after a resume of the same session they are unchanged.

title: pan-1787: sendEscapeKeyAsync tmux helper
description:
Per PRD WI-4 step 2: add `export async function sendEscapeKeyAsync(sessionName: string, times = 1): Promise<void>` to src/lib/tmux.ts. For each press run tmux send-keys targeting the exact pane (use the existing exactPaneTarget/exactSession helpers at tmux.ts:295-316 to avoid prefix-matching the wrong session) with the literal key name `Escape`, then wait 250ms between presses via `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 250))`. Async-only (repo rule: never add sync tmux callers); use the same async exec pattern as neighbouring async helpers (e.g. isHarnessProcessAlive at :509). Unit test with vi.useFakeTimers(): assert N invocations of the tmux command with Escape and the 250ms gaps advanced via advanceTimersByTimeAsync — never real waits.
- AC: sendEscapeKeyAsync(session, 2) sends exactly two tmux send-keys Escape commands to the exact-match pane target with a 250ms gap (fake-timer unit test)
- AC: The helper contains no execSync/spawnSync and its tests use fake timers only (no real waits)
close_reason: done

metadata.acJustification: Single small helper; two criteria cover behavior and repo-rule compliance.

title: pan-1778: Implement issue
description:
## Problem

Long remote runs outlive the synced OAuth token. When the host's Claude Code refreshes its token, the VM's synced copy is orphaned (refresh tokens rotate) and the agent stalls on `API Error: 401 · Please run /login`. On PAN-1762 run 1 the agent sat stalled for ~1h, which then cascaded into the reap-patrol kill and total work loss (see PAN-1676 incident comment, 2026-06-11).

A **reactive** heal landed in 67530a96f: the reap patrol greps the pane tail for the 401 stall, re-syncs credentials, and nudges the session. That only fires at patrol cadence after the stall has already begun — the agent still loses minutes-to-an-hour of wall clock per occurrence.

## Fix

Proactive refresh: a deacon patrol step (or a timer inside the reap patrol loop) that, for every active remote agent in `listActiveRemoteAgentStates()`, periodically re-runs `fly.syncClaudeCredentials(vmName)` — e.g. every 15 minutes, or whenever the host credentials file mtime changes (the cheap, precise trigger: watch `~/.claude/.credentials.json` and push on change).

Notes for implementation:
- `syncClaudeCredentials` already exists on FlyProvider (src/lib/remote/fly-provider.ts) and is idempotent.
- mtime-watch beats fixed cadence: tokens rotate when the HOST refreshes, which is observable locally without any VM round-trip. Fall back to a periodic push if the watch is awkward in the patrol structure.
- Keep the reactive heal (healStaleRemoteAuth in src/lib/remote/remote-completion.ts) as the backstop; this issue removes the common path to it.
- Cheap no-op when no remote agents are active (local file scan only — same guard the reap patrol uses in deacon.ts).

## Acceptance
- With a remote agent running, rotating host credentials results in the VM's credentials file being updated without operator action, before the agent hits a 401.
- No fly API calls when zero remote agents are active.
- Unit tests use fake timers per the repo testing rules.

Refs: PAN-1676 (epic + incident report), 67530a96f (reactive heal).
- AC: syncClaudeCredentials already exists on FlyProvider (src/lib/remote/fly-provider.ts) and is idempotent.
- AC: mtime-watch beats fixed cadence: tokens rotate when the HOST refreshes, which is observable locally without any VM round-trip. Fall back to a periodic push if the watch is awkward in the patrol structure.
- AC: Keep the reactive heal (healStaleRemoteAuth in src/lib/remote/remote-completion.ts) as the backstop; this issue removes the common path to it.
- AC: Cheap no-op when no remote agents are active (local file scan only — same guard the reap patrol uses in deacon.ts).
- AC: With a remote agent running, rotating host credentials results in the VM's credentials file being updated without operator action, before the agent hits a 401.
- AC: No fly API calls when zero remote agents are active.
- AC: Unit tests use fake timers per the repo testing rules.
close_reason: Implemented proactive Fly remote Claude credential refresh in deacon patrol with mtime/fingerprint trigger, 15 minute fallback, zero-active no-op guard, and fake-timer unit coverage.

title: pan-1776: End-to-end verification (PAN-1769 recipe) + docs entry
description:
Close the loop per PRD §5 acceptance + §6 step 7. (1) Subprocess integration test: spawn the real supervisor, deliver via deliverViaPrimitives, assert the child PTY saw exactly one copy of the content plus one Enter, and the stdout transcript echo appears exactly once. (2) End-to-end test: spawn a fresh conversation, send a message via POST /api/conversations/:name/message, assert the transcript contains exactly one user record with exactly one copy of the text (replicating the PAN-1769 verification recipe), and the bridge log records path 'supervisor-primitives'. (3) Write the docs entry per the docs index (pan docs / docs/ conventions) covering: supervisor identity sidecar, staleness detection, pan supervisor status/refresh, the deacon patrol gate, the /v1 primitives protocol, and the legacy-coexistence story (legacy POST / retained; removal is a telemetry-gated follow-up).
- AC: Subprocess test proves single-copy delivery via primitives: one content copy + one Enter at the child, one transcript stdout echo
- AC: Conversation e2e: POST /api/conversations/:name/message lands exactly one user record with one copy of the text; bridge log shows supervisor-primitives
- AC: Docs entry added and indexed per the docs-index convention; npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all green at issue completion

Add SUPERVISOR_AUTO_REFRESH_KEY = 'supervisor.auto_refresh' with isSupervisorAutoRefreshEnabled() (default false) / setSupervisorAutoRefreshEnabled() to src/lib/database/app-settings.ts, following the isMergeTrainEnabled pattern exactly (Effect getter, DatabaseError → false). Register a patrol in the deacon tick (alongside recoverOrphanedAgents / autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents, src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts ~4905): when enabled, refresh at most ONE stale supervisor per tick, oldest startedAt first, scope strictly agent-* (NEVER conversations — unsubmitted composer state cannot be detected), reusing the supervisor-refresh.ts core so idle/pause/troubled/Docker gates and skip-reason logging are identical to the CLI. Respect the global deacon freeze (deacon.globally_paused) and PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME.

title: pan-1776: deliverAgentMessage capability routing: primitives-v1 → server-side engine, else legacy POST
description:
In the supervisor tier of deliverAgentMessage (src/lib/agents.ts:1396-1426): first tryGetInfo(socketPath, token, 1_000) — returns null on ANY error (404 from old supervisor, timeout, connection refused). If info?.capabilities?.includes('primitives-v1'): await deliverViaPrimitives({...}) and return { ok: true, path: 'supervisor-primitives' }. Otherwise fall through to the existing legacy postUnixSocketJson with the 8s timeout (keep the worst-case-budget comment intact). A deliverViaPrimitives throw sets supervisorFailure and falls down the ladder exactly like a legacy socket-post failure (purge already left the composer clean, so the tmux fallback stays single-copy-safe). Extend the DeliveryResult path union and bridge-log values with 'supervisor-primitives'.
- AC: Test: fake socket advertising primitives-v1 ⇒ delivery goes through deliverViaPrimitives and the bridge log records path 'supervisor-primitives'
- AC: Test: fake socket returning 404 on /v1/info (old supervisor) ⇒ delivery uses the legacy POST / path with the existing 8s budget, path 'supervisor'
- AC: Test: deliverViaPrimitives failure falls through to the next ladder tier with supervisorFailure recorded, same as a legacy socket-post failure today

title: pan-1776: pan supervisor refresh <id> / --all-stale: gated rolling refresh with verification
description:
Implement the refresh core as an exported function in a new src/lib/supervisor-refresh.ts (shared later by the A4 patrol), with CLI subcommands `pan supervisor refresh <id> [--force]` and `pan supervisor refresh --all-stale [--max <n>]` (default max 3). Per-session semantics in order (PRD §A3): (1) SKIP with printed reason if not claude-code, Docker workspace, paused or troubled gate in state.json, or not stale (unless --force); (2) IDLE GATE — agents require idle via the runtime-mirror signal used by waitForAgentIdle (src/lib/agents.ts:763); conversations require summarizeConversationActivity(...).isWorking === false (src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-service.ts:836); --force overrides; NEVER interrupt a mid-turn session; (3) ROLL — agents: existing stop path then resumeAgent (regenerates launcher → new supervisor; auto-continue is PAN-1700 landing-verified); conversations: the internal function behind POST /api/conversations/:name/resume — call it directly, do not shell out to HTTP; (4) VERIFY — poll getSupervisorStatus until stale === false, 60s timeout, report per-session success/failure. --all-stale processes strictly sequentially (resume storms are how PAN-821-style thrash starts), capped by --max.
- AC: Integration test: refresh on a stale idle test conversation ends with getSupervisorStatus stale === false and a landed auto-continue (transcript check per PAN-1700 machinery)
- AC: Paused, troubled, mid-turn, Docker, and non-claude-code sessions are skipped with explicit printed reasons (unit tests over the gate logic, fake timers for any waits)
- AC: --all-stale processes sessions sequentially with --max cap (default 3); --force bypasses staleness and idle gates for a single explicit <id> only
- AC: Conversation roll goes through the internal resume function (no HTTP self-call), and agent roll goes through the existing stop→resumeAgent path

title: pan-1776: Server-side delivery engine: deliverViaPrimitives in pty-delivery-client.ts
description:
New src/lib/channels/pty-delivery-client.ts per PRD §B4: export async deliverViaPrimitives({socketPath, token, agentId, content, echoToStdout, caller?}) — throws on failure. Algorithm is the exact injectPtyMessage state machine over HTTP primitives, importing every constant/helper from pty-echo.ts: (1) GET /v1/info → baseOffset = outputEndOffset; (2) prefix = confirmationPrefix(trimmed), empty prefix skips to Enter; (3) attempts 1..INPUT_ECHO_CONFIRM_ATTEMPTS: attempt>1 first purges (POST /v1/write purgeBackspaces(len)) + sleep INPUT_PURGE_SETTLE_MS, then POST /v1/write {data: trimmedContent, echoToStdout} (transcript echo rides the CONTENT write), then poll GET /v1/output?since=baseOffset every INTERVAL_MS until TIMEOUT_MS — confirmed when normalizeForEchoMatch(accumulated) contains prefix OR PASTE_PLACEHOLDER_RE matches stripAnsi(accumulated); (4) unconfirmed → purge once more, log, throw (ladder falls back to tmux; purge keeps that single-copy-safe); (5) sleep INPUT_SETTLE_MS then POST /v1/write {data: '\r'} WITHOUT stdout echo; (6) appendChannelDeliveryLog with path 'supervisor-primitives'. Each primitive HTTP call timeout ≤ 2s; total engine budget must respect the ladder invariant documented at src/lib/agents.ts:1405 (client budget > worst-case write/confirm path).
- AC: Unit test vs fake unix-socket shim, happy path: exactly one content write (with echoToStdout), confirmation via /v1/output polling, then exactly one '\r' write — assert the exact ordered write sequence
- AC: Late-echo retry test: echo appears only after the purged second attempt — assert exact write sequence content → purge → content → '\r' (one purge between writes, single final submit)
- AC: Full-failure test: never-confirming output ⇒ exactly 2 purges total, deliverViaPrimitives throws, and NO '\r' is ever written
- AC: Placeholder confirmation test: '[Pasted text #N' output confirms without prefix match
- AC: All delay/poll paths use vi.useFakeTimers + advanceTimersByTimeAsync (fake-timers rule); no real setTimeout waits in unit tests

title: pan-1776: pan supervisor status subcommand + skills-lint exclusion entry
description:
New command file src/cli/commands/supervisor.ts registered in src/cli/index.ts as a subcommand group (follow the existing `pan backup` group pattern: program.command('supervisor') with nested .command('status')). `pan supervisor status` prints a table: id | kind (agent/conv) | sha (8 chars) | stale | idle | gates (paused/troubled), built from getSupervisorStatus across agent-* and conv-* state dirs plus the idle/gate signals (state.json paused/troubled fields; agents idle via the runtime-mirror signal behind waitForAgentIdle, conversations via summarizeConversationActivity). Per docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md, add `supervisor` to the exclusion list (line ~48) instead of writing a wrapper skill — --help is self-explanatory — and keep scripts/lint-skills.sh (runs in npm run lint) green.

- AC: npm run lint passes — the supervisor verb is on the SKILLS-CONVENTION exclusion list with a rationale, and lint-skills.sh stays green

Enumerate sessions by scanning ~/.panopticon/agents/*/ for pty sockets / supervisor.json and calling getSupervisorStatus.

title: pan-1776: Surface supervisorStale/supervisorSha in agent + conversation status payloads
description:
Add optional supervisorStale?: boolean and supervisorSha?: string to the agent status payload (src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts status route) and to the conversation payload (src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, computed in the same place sessionAlive is, ~line 1987). Populate from getSupervisorStatus ONLY for claude-code sessions that have a supervisor socket; omit the fields entirely otherwise. Keep the lookups async and cheap (the dist-sha is cached by a2-status-module).
