title: pan-1776: Socket protocol v1: URL-path routing + /v1/info, /v1/write, /v1/output, /v1/resize
description:
Extend createPtySupervisorServer() (src/lib/channels/pty-supervisor.ts:319) from POST-/-only to routing by req.method + url.pathname per PRD §B1 table. Legacy POST / stays byte-for-byte compatible ({content, echo?, caller?, meta?} → injectPtyMessage). New: GET /v1/info → {schemaVersion, pid, scriptSha256, capabilities, outputEndOffset}; POST /v1/write {data, echoToStdout?} → raw child.write(data), echoToStdout mirrors to supervisor stdout exactly like payload.echo does today, returns {ok: true, outputEndOffset}; GET /v1/output?since=<n> → ring slice {startOffset, endOffset, data}; POST /v1/resize {cols, rows} → child.resize bounds-checked 1..1000. Wire ONE child.onData subscription in main() appending to an OutputRing instance. All endpoints require the existing x-pan-pty-token header; unknown paths → 404; socket stays mode 0600 with no new listening surface. Append "primitives-v1" to the shared capabilities constant (sidecar + /v1/info now advertise ["legacy-post", "primitives-v1"]).
- AC: Subprocess integration tests: /v1/info returns identity + monotonically increasing outputEndOffset; /v1/write injects raw bytes into the child PTY and mirrors to stdout when echoToStdout; /v1/output?since returns the slice with correct offsets; /v1/resize resizes within bounds and rejects 0 or >1000
- AC: All /v1 endpoints reject requests without a matching pty token; unknown paths return 404; legacy POST / behavior is unchanged (all 12 pre-existing tests still green)
- AC: Sidecar and /v1/info both advertise capabilities ["legacy-post", "primitives-v1"] from a single shared constant

title: pan-1776: supervisor-status.ts: getSupervisorStatus + getCurrentDistSha
description:
New module src/lib/supervisor-status.ts per PRD §A2: export interface SupervisorStatus { running, identityPresent, stale, capabilities, runningSha?, distSha? }; export async getSupervisorStatus(agentId); export async getCurrentDistSha() — sha256 of resolvePtySupervisorScriptPath() (src/lib/agents.ts:1766), cached by (path, mtimeMs). running = socket exists AND identity pid alive (process.kill(pid, 0) in try/catch). stale = running && (no identity || scriptSha256 !== current dist sha) — a running socket with no supervisor.json is a pre-PAN-1776 supervisor: legacy-only AND unknown version, treated as stale. capabilities = [] when unknown. All IO async; this module is server-reachable so no execSync anywhere.
- AC: Unit tests with tmpdir fixtures cover: missing supervisor.json (running socket ⇒ stale, capabilities []), dead pid (running false), sha match (stale false), sha mismatch (stale true)
- AC: getCurrentDistSha caches by (path, mtimeMs): second call with unchanged mtime does not re-read the file; mtime change invalidates (testable via injected/fixture path)

title: pan-1776: Extract shared echo/purge helpers into src/lib/channels/pty-echo.ts (pure move)
description:
Pure move per PRD §B3: relocate the delivery constants (INPUT_ECHO_CONFIRM_TIMEOUT_MS/INTERVAL_MS/ATTEMPTS/PREFIX_CHARS, INPUT_SETTLE_MS, INPUT_PURGE_MAX_CHARS, INPUT_PURGE_SETTLE_MS, PASTE_PLACEHOLDER_RE — currently pty-supervisor.ts:35-41,223) and pure functions (stripAnsi, normalizePtyText, normalizeForEchoMatch, confirmationPrefix at :192-228, plus a purgeBackspaces(charCount) helper extracted from the existing purge logic at :254 — '\x7f'.repeat(min(n, MAX) + 8)) into new src/lib/channels/pty-echo.ts, exported. pty-supervisor.ts imports them; behavior identical. No logic changes — this is the one-source-of-truth that b4-delivery-engine will import server-side.
- AC: All 12 pre-existing pty-supervisor.test.ts tests pass unchanged (no test edits)
- AC: pty-echo.ts exports exactly the PRD §B3 surface (constants + stripAnsi, normalizePtyText, normalizeForEchoMatch, confirmationPrefix, purgeBackspaces, PASTE_PLACEHOLDER_RE) and pty-supervisor.ts contains no duplicate definitions

title: pan-1776: OutputRing: 64KiB ring buffer with absolute offsets
description:
Per PRD §B2: export class OutputRing { append(s: string): void; slice(since: number): {startOffset, endOffset, data} } in src/lib/channels/pty-supervisor.ts. Capacity 64 KiB; tracks absoluteOffset = total bytes ever seen. slice(since) returns from max(since, bufferStart) — when since < buffer start, return from buffer start so the caller detects the gap via startOffset > since. Pure class, no IO.
- AC: Direct unit tests cover: append+slice happy path, wrap-around past 64KiB preserving absolute offsets, gap behavior (since < bufferStart ⇒ startOffset > since), and empty buffer (start === end, data '')

title: pan-1776: Supervisor identity sidecar (supervisor.json) + computeScriptSha256
description:
In src/lib/channels/pty-supervisor.ts, write ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/supervisor.json immediately after the socket binds, atomically (write tmp + rename), per PRD §A1: { schemaVersion: 1, pid, scriptPath (process.argv[1]), scriptSha256 (sha256 of the file at process.argv[1] read at startup), capabilities, startedAt }. capabilities is a module-level constant set to ["legacy-post"] in this bead (b1-socket-endpoints later appends "primitives-v1"). Implement async writeSupervisorIdentity(agentId) — best-effort, never throws (same posture as the log appenders) — and export computeScriptSha256(path) for tests. Do NOT delete the file on shutdown: staleness checks validate liveness independently and the stale file is useful post-mortem. The rebuild-between-load-and-hash race is tolerable; document it in a comment, don't engineer around it.
- AC: Integration test (existing subprocess harness in src/lib/channels/__tests__/pty-supervisor.test.ts) spawns the supervisor and asserts supervisor.json exists with schemaVersion 1, the supervisor's pid, capabilities ["legacy-post"], and scriptSha256 matching computeScriptSha256 of the spawned script
- AC: computeScriptSha256 is exported and unit-tested (64-hex-char sha256 of file contents)
- AC: Sidecar write is atomic (tmp + rename), never throws on failure, and is NOT removed on SIGTERM/shutdown (assert file survives the existing unlinks-socket-on-SIGTERM test flow)
